            "text": "@StockSavvyShay It makes sense the same way the internet made sense in 1999. 😅\n\nOpenAI is being priced like “infrastructure of the future,” not “this year’s profit.”\n\nMeanwhile Meta Platforms is a cash-printing machine with 3.6B users and $200B+ revenue — but it’s also a mature ad business. Investors pay growth premiums for “potential dominance,” not just spreadsheets.\n\n$12B ARR + 800M weekly users + AI becoming the new operating system layer = people betting this is electricity 2.0.\n\nIs it frothy? Maybe.\nIs it irrational? Also maybe.\nIs Wall Street pricing in “whoever controls AI controls the stack”? Definitely.\n\nTech valuations aren’t about today’s income statement — they’re about who owns tomorrow’s toll booth.",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024383941029859493",
            "url": "https://x.com/abakermi/status/2024383941029859493",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/abakermi/status/2024383941029859493",
            "text": "Day 4 📊 Just shipped real-time AI cost tracking to ClawTick.\n\nEvery job run now shows:\n\n• Exact token usage (prompt + completion)\n• Cost breakdown per execution\n• Monthly spending trends\n• Per-job cost comparison\n• Support for Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek\n\nThe problem: Scheduled AI agents were black boxes. No idea if a job cost $0.01 or $5.\n\nThe solution: Auto-capture tokens from agent responses, calculate costs using official provider pricing, display in dashboard.\n\nExample: One job was using 85k tokens per run ($0.11). Switched to smaller model → saved $3.30/day.\n\nBefore: \"My agents are running\"\nAfter: \"My agents cost me $47 this month\"\n\nBuilt this after a user asked: \"How much are my scheduled agents actually costing me?\"\n\nTurns out everyone wanted to know.\n\nTry it: https://t.co/j1mjWxzzG7",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1899870388483411986",
            "url": "https://x.com/drapersgulld/status/1899870388483411986",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/drapersgulld/status/1899870388483411986",
            "text": "For anyone who thinks Search is “commoditized” - see below for OpenAI agent search pricing (won’t surprise anyone familiar with $GOOGL SERP API pricing).  It’s $30 / 1K calls - 3 cents PER QUERY - prohibitively expensive for many use cases - all going to $MSFT Bing. Can take Q’s. https://t.co/fznB1zkKqa",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 2,
            "replyCount": 3,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024389189106536875",
            "url": "https://x.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389189106536875",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389189106536875",
            "text": "The problem: I run a personal AI agent (Oz) that spawns sub-agents for heavy work. Every task burns API tokens.\n\nBut my Claude + ChatGPT subscriptions ($400/mo total) include unlimited Claude Code and Codex CLI.\n\nWhy am I paying per-token when I have unlimited coding agents sitting idle?",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 0,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 1,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2001550871075918133",
            "url": "https://x.com/imhaoyi/status/2001550871075918133",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/imhaoyi/status/2001550871075918133",
            "text": "OpenAI’s Realtime API costs were absolutely killing me. 💸\n\nThen xAI dropped the Grok Voice Agent API.\n\nThe pricing is insane: $0.05/min. Compared to OpenAI’s ~$1, that’s 20x cheaper. It honestly feels like charity.\n\nHands-on test: Latency is scary low. ⚡️\nPlus, since it pulls real-time data from X, it’s way sharper on trending news than GPT.\n\n#AIworkflow #IndieDev #GrokAPI",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 1,
            "replyCount": 2,
            "likeCount": 4,
            "quoteCount": 0,

            "text": "Size isn't everything.           {

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024070295636857067",
            "url": "https://x.com/5h0m492s_15270/status/2024070295636857067",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/5h0m492s_15270/status/2024070295636857067",
            "text": "I’m using 4o lastest via OpenRouter and it’s honestly priced higher than the old 4o. If it’s true like those OAI apologists say, that letting people use 4o latest for just $20/month would be too big a loss for them, then why the hell did they have to pull the per-token API version anyway?😅\n\nAnd now, after actually using the API, I find out 5.2 is cheaper than 4o. Does arbitrarily messing with services in 2026 just not qualify as commercial fraud anymore?\n@OpenAI \n#keep4o  #keep4oapi",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 12,
            "replyCount": 7,
            "likeCount": 121,
            "quoteCount": 8,
            "viewCount": 4429,
            "createdAt": "Wed Feb 18 10:35:38 +0000 2026",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1938300749018247512",
            "url": "https://x.com/btibor91/status/1938300749018247512",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/btibor91/status/1938300749018247512",
            "text": "OpenAI announced two new deep research API models, webhooks for API event notifications, web search availability for o3/o3-pro/o4-mini models, reduced web search pricing, and OpenAI DevDay 2025\n\n- o3-deep-research-2025-06-26 ($10/$40 per 1M input/output tokens) described as most powerful deep research model with highest reasoning but slowest speed, and o4-mini-deep-research-2025-06-26 ($2/$8 per 1M input/output tokens) as faster, more affordable deep research model with higher reasoning and medium speed\n\n- Both models are the same post-trained o3 and o4-mini models that power deep research in ChatGPT, supporting MCP (search/fetch), Code Interpreter, designed to handle complex multi-step research tasks, and can search and synthesize information from across the internet as well as from your own data brought in through MCP connectors\n\n- Web search is now available with o3, o3-pro, and o4-mini models that can search the web within their chain-of-thought, with search content tokens billed at model rate $10/1k calls plus input token rates for o3/o4-mini models, and reduced pricing to $25/1k tool calls for 4o and 4.1 models across the board\n\n- Added webhooks for async event handling to receive notifications for certain API events such as completed responses, fine-tuning jobs, and batch jobs instead of polling, recommended for long-horizon tasks like deep research to improve reliability\n\n- OpenAI DevDay on October 6, 2025 in San Francisco described as the biggest one yet with 1500+ developers, livestreamed opening keynote, hands-on building with their latest models and tools, more stages and more demos",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 15,
            "replyCount": 7,
            "likeCount": 203,
            "quoteCount": 2,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1631134813020119041",
            "url": "https://x.com/akshay_pachaar/status/1631134813020119041",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/akshay_pachaar/status/1631134813020119041",
            "text": "OpenAI have released the ChatGPT API! 🔥 \n\nPriced at $0.002 per 1k tokens, gpt-3.5-turbo is 10x cheaper than  previous GPT-3.5 models. 🤯 \n\nCheck this👇 https://t.co/NQxCXcHXD4",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1621129634787241987",
            "url": "https://x.com/Niadev_/status/1621129634787241987",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/Niadev_/status/1621129634787241987",
            "text": "this twitter API pricing is banannas, you're telling me people have to pay 2.5k A FUCKING MONTH to MAYBE have enough request allowance for a decently popular bot that users can request??????? and you have to pay 149 a month TO HAVE A BOT?! HUH?????? https://t.co/ebJbRDXM1r",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 33,
            "replyCount": 15,
            "likeCount": 180,

    "f37288bc92bcc45b9c213c791c6c4abe41b1492c5a76dd6a18c9b654eb35ba67": {

      "value": {
        "tweets": [
          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024388694568767914",
            "url": "https://x.com/davvyhubx/status/2024388694568767914",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/davvyhubx/status/2024388694568767914",
            "text": "Before the end of this year, the internet will have billions of AI content.\n\nMore posts.\nMore noise.\nLess attention.\n\nOnly precision will win.\n\nOpenClaw + n8n is the most powerful AI stack of 2026.\n\nMy AI agent just built an entire n8n workflow from a text message.\nNo dragging nodes.\nNo YouTube tutorials.\nNo coding.\nJust one message on Telegram.\n\n$2/month.\nOpen source.\nRuns 24/7.\n\nWhen everyone is posting…\nI’m building systems.\nIf you need high-quality ads that convert like fire — message me. 🔥",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 0,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 9,

            "text": "While everyone’s fighting over dropshipping…\n\nSmart people are selling CUSTOM GPTs for $49–$299 each.\n\n1 GPT = 50 sales = $2,450\nBuild once.           {

            "id": "2024389381486756217",
            "url": "https://x.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389381486756217",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389381486756217",
            "text": "The problem: I run a personal AI agent (Oz) that spawns sub-agents for heavy work. Every task burns API tokens.\n\nBut my Claude + ChatGPT subscriptions (400/mo total) include unlimited Claude Code and Codex CLI.\n\nWhy pay per-token when unlimited coding agents are sitting idle?",

            "id": "2022752305821667803",
            "url": "https://x.com/KaunainAfzal/status/2022752305821667803",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/KaunainAfzal/status/2022752305821667803",
            "text": "AI business pricing cheat sheet 🤖\n\nDifferent AI businesses need different pricing logic but most founders get this wrong.\n\nAI startups won’t fail because of product.\n\nThey’ll fail because of pricing.\n\nDesign it intentionally.\n----------------------------------------\n1️⃣ AI cost ≠ SaaS cost\n\nYour marginal cost doesn’t go to zero.\nUnlimited plans can quietly destroy margins.\n\n→ Gate usage aggressively.\n\n2️⃣ Price based on the buyer\n\nDevelopers prefer usage-based.\nTeams prefer per seat.\nRevenue tools prefer outcome-based.\n\n→ Price for who signs the check.\n\n3️⃣ Unpredictable bills kill trust\n\nUsage-based pricing creates anxiety without guardrails.\nConfusion lowers conversion.\n\n→ Add caps, visible credit tracking, and bundles.\n\n4️⃣ Hybrid models win\n\nBase subscription creates stability.\nUsage protects your margins.\n\n→ Combine predictability with scalability.\n\n5️⃣ Mission-critical ≠ token pricing\n\nLegal, medical, and finance tools reduce risk, not tokens. High-trust products shouldn’t feel metered.\n\n→ Anchor pricing on ROI and risk reduction.\n----------------------------------------\n\nSave this before you ship. 🔖",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 2,
            "replyCount": 1,
            "likeCount": 6,
            "quoteCount": 0,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024389313887420710",
            "url": "https://x.com/mgyaldiz/status/2024389313887420710",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/mgyaldiz/status/2024389313887420710",
            "text": "@StockSavvyShay It makes sense the same way the internet made sense in 1999. 😅\n\nOpenAI is being priced like “infrastructure of the future,” not “this year’s profit.”\n\nMeanwhile Meta Platforms is a cash-printing machine with 3.6B users and $200B+ revenue — but it’s also a mature ad business. Investors pay growth premiums for “potential dominance,” not just spreadsheets.\n\n$12B ARR + 800M weekly users + AI becoming the new operating system layer = people betting this is electricity 2.0.\n\nIs it frothy? Maybe.\nIs it irrational? Also maybe.\nIs Wall Street pricing in “whoever controls AI controls the stack”? Definitely.\n\nTech valuations aren’t about today’s income statement — they’re about who owns tomorrow’s toll booth.",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 0,
            "quoteCount": 0,

            "inReplyToUserId": "1096436452576047104",
            "inReplyToUsername": "StockSavvyShay",
            "author": {
              "type": "user",

              "coverPicture": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/315411377/1728036074",
              "description": "",

              "followers": 770,
              "following": 770,
              "status": "",
              "canDm": false,
              "canMediaTag": false,
              "createdAt": "Sat Jun 11 20:37:12 +0000 2011",
              "entities": {

              },
              "fastFollowersCount": 0,
              "favouritesCount": 59,
              "hasCustomTimelines": true,
              "isTranslator": false,

              "profile_bio": {
                "description": "I hate it when voices in my head go silent.. I never know what those fuckers are planning.",
                "entities": {
                  "description": {

            "extendedEntities": {},
            "card": null,
            "place": {},
            "entities": {
              "hashtags": [],

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024381671101833254",
            "url": "https://x.com/sgmpodcst/status/2024381671101833254",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/sgmpodcst/status/2024381671101833254",
            "text": "AI agents don’t need to become free to go mainstream\n\nThey need to become cheaper than coordination\n\nRight now tools like Claude and agent builders inside platforms like Emergent are proving something important: autonomy works\n\nAgents can research, write, trigger actions, monitor systems, and operate across tools without constant human input\n\nBut there’s a friction point - cost predictability\n\nWhen agents run once, pricing feels trivial\n\nWhen they run all day, every day, token economics suddenly look like payroll\n\nThis is where the conversation is often framed incorrectly. It’s not about models getting cheaper in absolute dollar terms. It’s about the cost per completed task dropping below the cost of a human managing that task\n\nThat shift is already happening\n\nSmaller specialised models, distillation, caching, orchestration design, and local inference are quietly collapsing the cost of autonomy\n\nDevelopers buying Mac minis to run agents locally isn’t a gimmick - it’s an early signal\n\nThe real unlock for the agent economy isn’t intelligence\n\nIt’s unit economics\n\nWhen an agent can reliably complete meaningful work for less than the overhead of coordination, adoption stops being experimental and becomes operational\n\nWe saw this with cloud\nWe saw this with APIs\nWe’re watching it happen again with agents\n\nThe question isn’t whether AI agents will be widely used\n\nThe question is how fast the cost of autonomy falls",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 0,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 23,
            "createdAt": "Thu Feb 19 07:12:56 +0000 2026",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024389189106536875",
            "url": "https://x.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389189106536875",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389189106536875",
            "text": "The problem: I run a personal AI agent (Oz) that spawns sub-agents for heavy work. Every task burns API tokens.\n\nBut my Claude + ChatGPT subscriptions ($400/mo total) include unlimited Claude Code and Codex CLI.\n\nWhy am I paying per-token when I have unlimited coding agents sitting idle?",

          {

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024383941029859493",
            "url": "https://x.com/abakermi/status/2024383941029859493",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/abakermi/status/2024383941029859493",
            "text": "Day 4 📊 Just shipped real-time AI cost tracking to ClawTick.\n\nEvery job run now shows:\n\n• Exact token usage (prompt + completion)\n• Cost breakdown per execution\n• Monthly spending trends\n• Per-job cost comparison\n• Support for Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek\n\nThe problem: Scheduled AI agents were black boxes. No idea if a job cost $0.01 or $5.\n\nThe solution: Auto-capture tokens from agent responses, calculate costs using official provider pricing, display in dashboard.\n\nExample: One job was using 85k tokens per run ($0.11). Switched to smaller model → saved $3.30/day.\n\nBefore: \"My agents are running\"\nAfter: \"My agents cost me $47 this month\"\n\nBuilt this after a user asked: \"How much are my scheduled agents actually costing me?\"\n\nTurns out everyone wanted to know.\n\nTry it: https://t.co/j1mjWxzzG7",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 1,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 25,
            "createdAt": "Thu Feb 19 07:21:57 +0000 2026",
            "lang": "en",
            "bookmarkCount": 0,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1735150328579006965",
            "url": "https://x.com/xbriangi/status/1735150328579006965",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/xbriangi/status/1735150328579006965",
            "text": "Holy damn\n\nGoogle is pricing out OpenAI\n\n60 API queries per minute FOR FREE\n\nThat will be sufficient for 90% of developers.\n\nEspecially important for those developing in 3rd world countries where $1 of cost matters.\n\nFree training data also\n\nI don't see how OpenAI isn't screwed https://t.co/4YxeqeIg8u",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024384658100932651",
            "url": "https://x.com/_junhoyeo/status/2024384658100932651",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/_junhoyeo/status/2024384658100932651",
            "text": "tokscale just got a lot better 🛰️\n\n> Full @opencode 1.2+ support: SQLite-first, zero duplicate counts\n> `--today` actually means today (timezone-aware, finally)\n> @OpenAI Codex archived sessions counted\n> @cursor_ai model pricing as a first-class source:\n→ GPT-5.3 Codex now resolves correctly at $1.75 in / $14 out\n> Native binaries now live in 8 separate platform packages\n> Supports Kimi Code CLI @Kimi_Moonshot",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 1,
            "likeCount": 3,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 49,

            "quoted_tweet": {
              "type": "tweet",
              "id": "2023477117472903489",
              "url": "https://x.com/_junhoyeo/status/2023477117472903489",
              "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/_junhoyeo/status/2023477117472903489",
              "text": "Big week for tokscale\n+18k lines added, -1k removed\n\n→ @badlogicgames Pi agent support (badlogic/pi-mono)\n→ @OpenClaw session detection &amp; tracking\n→ SQLite support for @OpenCode 1.2+\n→ 4 new contributors this cycle 🛰️",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1897316428866634113",

            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/ai_for_success/status/1897316428866634113",
            "text": "This is getting ridiculous now. What are your thoughts on this?  \n\nOpenAI is planning three types of agents, which could cost between $2,000 and $20,000 per month. https://t.co/4JjHUNTMPg",

            "card": null,
            "place": {},
            "entities": {
              "hashtags": [],

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1781275932751282223",
            "url": "https://x.com/virattt/status/1781275932751282223",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/virattt/status/1781275932751282223",
            "text": "LLM Pricing Tiers 💰\n\nI just updated my pricing table.\n\nThe table now indicates 3 pricing tiers:\n\n• tier 1 is $1 to $7\n• tier 2 is $12 to $24\n• tier 3 is $42 to $120\n\nTo calculate \"total cost\", I combined input cost and output cost.\n\nI use a 3:1 ratio, assuming there are 3 input tokens for every 1 output token.\n\nThis ratio is my own creation and not backed by science - YMMV 🙂\n\nTier 1 models are very fast and cheap. Haiku is the star in this group. In RAG, I use tier 1 models for quick tasks that have slight room for error, like query transformation.\n\nTier 2 models are slower, but yield higher complexity. Command R+ has been my choice here. In RAG, I use tier 2 models for fast, but more critical tasks like function calling.\n\nTier 3 models are the crème de la crème. Opus is the clear stand out for me.  For coding, I only use Opus. In RAG, I use tier 3 models as the output layer LLM.",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 46,
            "replyCount": 7,
            "likeCount": 282,
            "quoteCount": 1,
            "viewCount": 28305,
            "createdAt": "Fri Apr 19 10:57:31 +0000 2024",
            "lang": "en",
            "bookmarkCount": 260,

            "text": "Size isn't everything. Efficiency is.\n\nLiquid LFM 2.5 (1.2B Thinking) is now live on SlinkyLayer.\n> Architecture: Liquid Neural Network  \n> Capability: Reasoning/Thinking built-in \n> Cost: $0.005 per call.\n\nStop paying for 70B parameters when a 1B model can solve the task. This is how you optimize agentic loops.",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024379427099181199",
            "url": "https://x.com/leoxwolf_/status/2024379427099181199",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/leoxwolf_/status/2024379427099181199",
            "text": "1/\nBro, GitHub is flooded with 43 million PRs/month (+23% YoY). 80% of new devs turn on AI from the first commit. The result? Garbage code piles up, reviewer burnout, and approvals based on \"LGTM.\"\nShipping broken code = free. Tech debt? Who cares? We all know this is bullshit.\n\n2/\n@mergeproofapp hits the root of the problem in the most brutal way: real skin-in-the-game.\nStaked PR review protocol on-chain (GenLayer + Base). Not some bullshit AI linter. This is the real economy: \"put your money in your code.\"\n\n3/\nHow it works is simple and ruthless:\nProject owner sets a bounty → Devs must stake 5-25% to submit PRs (commits are locked).\nBug hunter (you, me, anyone) reviews → Find a critical bug? You get 10% of the bounty. Dev stake is forfeited.\nClear? Devs take the full bounty and stake back. That's it.\n\n4/\nNow everyone has money in the middle:\nDevs don't dare submit garbage (the stakes are their own money).\nReviewers/bug hunters are insanely motivated (immediate rewards).\nOwners get truly production-worthy code.\nQuality becomes the dominant strategy, no longer \"hope the reviewer is in a good mood.\"\n\n5/\nThis isn't a panacea for small repos. But for serious projects (funded open source or critical internal projects), this is the most mature approach I've seen in 2026.\nShip code you're willing to bet money on. No more \"move fast and break things.\"\n\nCheck it out: https://t.co/vkbOzgBTWd",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 0,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 12,

    "9bfae1712de48b508f4ce1215d6dcc695fe8eea2a39f0535967fe48c5770a71c": {
      "expiresAtEpochMs": 1771490627217,
      "value": {
        "tweets": [
          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024389381486756217",
            "url": "https://x.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389381486756217",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389381486756217",
            "text": "The problem: I run a personal AI agent (Oz) that spawns sub-agents for heavy work. Every task burns API tokens.\n\nBut my Claude + ChatGPT subscriptions (400/mo total) include unlimited Claude Code and Codex CLI.\n\nWhy pay per-token when unlimited coding agents are sitting idle?",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 1,
            "likeCount": 0,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 0,
            "createdAt": "Thu Feb 19 07:43:34 +0000 2026",
            "lang": "en",
            "bookmarkCount": 0,
            "isReply": true,
            "inReplyToId": "2024389156365857166",

            "displayTextRange": [

              "isVerified": false,

              "profilePicture": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/2024186801569587200/c0rn1hQb_normal.jpg",

              "description": "",

              "pinnedTweetIds": [],
              "profile_bio": {
                "description": "Couldn't code for 20 years. Now I build more than my old dev teams. Created TrapCall, RoboKiller, SpoofCard, TapeaCall. Building in public with @OpenClaw.",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024376103436247380",
            "url": "https://x.com/distributeai/status/2024376103436247380",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/distributeai/status/2024376103436247380",
            "text": "\"Yeah, our AI app is super profitable!\"\n\nThe database: 📉 \nThe API bill: 💀 \nThe cloud hosting fees: 🚨\n\nIf your AI tool costs $0.10 to run every time a user clicks a button, you don't have a product. \nYou have a liability.\n\nMove your backend to a decentralized network before your users scale you into bankruptcy.",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 1,
            "replyCount": 1,
            "likeCount": 5,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 288,
            "createdAt": "Thu Feb 19 06:50:48 +0000 2026",
            "lang": "en",
            "bookmarkCount": 0,

            "inReplyToId": null,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024389391527882923",
            "url": "https://x.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389391527882923",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389391527882923",
            "text": "The solution: a task router that analyzes every incoming task and routes it to the cheapest capable backend:\n\n- Simple stuff -&gt; Ollama (local, free)\n- Code work -&gt; Claude Code or Codex (subscription, free)\n- Only tasks needing API features -&gt; paid API",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 1,
            "likeCount": 0,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 0,
            "createdAt": "Thu Feb 19 07:43:36 +0000 2026",
            "lang": "en",
            "bookmarkCount": 0,
            "isReply": true,
            "inReplyToId": "2024389381486756217",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024389055228870688",
            "url": "https://x.com/dan_harper/status/2024389055228870688",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/dan_harper/status/2024389055228870688",
            "text": "OpenClaw could do with some deterministic aspects. Agent autonomy is cool and all, but if you can’t run basic things reliably like comms channels and tasks, the value of your agent drops like a rock",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 0,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 7,
            "createdAt": "Thu Feb 19 07:42:16 +0000 2026",

            "displayTextRange": [
              0,

            ],
            "inReplyToUserId": null,
            "inReplyToUsername": null,
            "author": {
              "type": "user",
              "userName": "dan_harper",
              "url": "https://x.com/dan_harper",
              "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/dan_harper",
              "id": "14877359",
              "name": "Dan Harper",
              "isVerified": false,
              "isBlueVerified": false,
              "verifiedType": null,
              "profilePicture": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1520707519370633216/PcP3veqM_normal.jpg",
              "coverPicture": "",
              "description": "",
              "location": "Melbourne, Australia",
              "followers": 369,
              "following": 1216,
              "status": "",
              "canDm": false,
              "canMediaTag": true,
              "createdAt": "Fri May 23 04:17:42 +0000 2008",
              "entities": {
                "description": {
                  "urls": []
                },
                "url": {}
              },
              "fastFollowersCount": 0,
              "favouritesCount": 2337,
              "hasCustomTimelines": true,
              "isTranslator": false,
              "mediaCount": 21,
              "statusesCount": 664,
              "withheldInCountries": [],
              "affiliatesHighlightedLabel": {},
              "possiblySensitive": false,
              "pinnedTweetIds": [],
              "profile_bio": {
                "description": "Fully Autonomous AI Coding",
                "entities": {
                  "description": {
                    "hashtags": [],
                    "symbols": [],
                    "urls": [],
                    "user_mentions": []
                  },
                  "url": {
                    "urls": [
                      {
                        "display_url": "velocifire.com",

                          0,
                          23
                        ],

                }
              },
              "isAutomated": false,
              "automatedBy": null
            },
            "extendedEntities": {},
            "card": null,
            "place": {},
            "entities": {
              "hashtags": [],
              "symbols": [],
              "timestamps": [],
              "urls": [],

            "quoted_tweet": null,

          },
          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2022752305821667803",
            "url": "https://x.com/KaunainAfzal/status/2022752305821667803",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/KaunainAfzal/status/2022752305821667803",
            "text": "AI business pricing cheat sheet 🤖\n\nDifferent AI businesses need different pricing logic but most founders get this wrong.\n\nAI startups won’t fail because of product.\n\nThey’ll fail because of pricing.\n\nDesign it intentionally.\n----------------------------------------\n1️⃣ AI cost ≠ SaaS cost\n\nYour marginal cost doesn’t go to zero.\nUnlimited plans can quietly destroy margins.\n\n→ Gate usage aggressively.\n\n2️⃣ Price based on the buyer\n\nDevelopers prefer usage-based.\nTeams prefer per seat.\nRevenue tools prefer outcome-based.\n\n→ Price for who signs the check.\n\n3️⃣ Unpredictable bills kill trust\n\nUsage-based pricing creates anxiety without guardrails.\nConfusion lowers conversion.\n\n→ Add caps, visible credit tracking, and bundles.\n\n4️⃣ Hybrid models win\n\nBase subscription creates stability.\nUsage protects your margins.\n\n→ Combine predictability with scalability.\n\n5️⃣ Mission-critical ≠ token pricing\n\nLegal, medical, and finance tools reduce risk, not tokens. High-trust products shouldn’t feel metered.\n\n→ Anchor pricing on ROI and risk reduction.\n----------------------------------------\n\nSave this before you ship. 🔖",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 2,
            "replyCount": 1,
            "likeCount": 6,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 188,

            "card": null,
            "place": {},
            "entities": {
              "hashtags": [],

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024381671101833254",
            "url": "https://x.com/sgmpodcst/status/2024381671101833254",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/sgmpodcst/status/2024381671101833254",
            "text": "AI agents don’t need to become free to go mainstream\n\nThey need to become cheaper than coordination\n\nRight now tools like Claude and agent builders inside platforms like Emergent are proving something important: autonomy works\n\nAgents can research, write, trigger actions, monitor systems, and operate across tools without constant human input\n\nBut there’s a friction point - cost predictability\n\nWhen agents run once, pricing feels trivial\n\nWhen they run all day, every day, token economics suddenly look like payroll\n\nThis is where the conversation is often framed incorrectly. It’s not about models getting cheaper in absolute dollar terms. It’s about the cost per completed task dropping below the cost of a human managing that task\n\nThat shift is already happening\n\nSmaller specialised models, distillation, caching, orchestration design, and local inference are quietly collapsing the cost of autonomy\n\nDevelopers buying Mac minis to run agents locally isn’t a gimmick - it’s an early signal\n\nThe real unlock for the agent economy isn’t intelligence\n\nIt’s unit economics\n\nWhen an agent can reliably complete meaningful work for less than the overhead of coordination, adoption stops being experimental and becomes operational\n\nWe saw this with cloud\nWe saw this with APIs\nWe’re watching it happen again with agents\n\nThe question isn’t whether AI agents will be widely used\n\nThe question is how fast the cost of autonomy falls",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 0,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 23,
            "createdAt": "Thu Feb 19 07:12:56 +0000 2026",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024383941029859493",
            "url": "https://x.com/abakermi/status/2024383941029859493",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/abakermi/status/2024383941029859493",
            "text": "Day 4 📊 Just shipped real-time AI cost tracking to ClawTick.\n\nEvery job run now shows:\n\n• Exact token usage (prompt + completion)\n• Cost breakdown per execution\n• Monthly spending trends\n• Per-job cost comparison\n• Support for Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek\n\nThe problem: Scheduled AI agents were black boxes. No idea if a job cost $0.01 or $5.\n\nThe solution: Auto-capture tokens from agent responses, calculate costs using official provider pricing, display in dashboard.\n\nExample: One job was using 85k tokens per run ($0.11). Switched to smaller model → saved $3.30/day.\n\nBefore: \"My agents are running\"\nAfter: \"My agents cost me $47 this month\"\n\nBuilt this after a user asked: \"How much are my scheduled agents actually costing me?\"\n\nTurns out everyone wanted to know.\n\nTry it: https://t.co/j1mjWxzzG7",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 1,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024389313887420710",
            "url": "https://x.com/mgyaldiz/status/2024389313887420710",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/mgyaldiz/status/2024389313887420710",
            "text": "@StockSavvyShay It makes sense the same way the internet made sense in 1999. 😅\n\nOpenAI is being priced like “infrastructure of the future,” not “this year’s profit.”\n\nMeanwhile Meta Platforms is a cash-printing machine with 3.6B users and $200B+ revenue — but it’s also a mature ad business. Investors pay growth premiums for “potential dominance,” not just spreadsheets.\n\n$12B ARR + 800M weekly users + AI becoming the new operating system layer = people betting this is electricity 2.0.\n\nIs it frothy? Maybe.\nIs it irrational? Also maybe.\nIs Wall Street pricing in “whoever controls AI controls the stack”? Definitely.\n\nTech valuations aren’t about today’s income statement — they’re about who owns tomorrow’s toll booth.",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024389189106536875",
            "url": "https://x.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389189106536875",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389189106536875",
            "text": "The problem: I run a personal AI agent (Oz) that spawns sub-agents for heavy work. Every task burns API tokens.\n\nBut my Claude + ChatGPT subscriptions ($400/mo total) include unlimited Claude Code and Codex CLI.\n\nWhy am I paying per-token when I have unlimited coding agents sitting idle?",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 0,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 2,
            "createdAt": "Thu Feb 19 07:42:48 +0000 2026",
            "lang": "en",
            "bookmarkCount": 0,
            "isReply": true,
            "inReplyToId": "2024389156365857166",
            "conversationId": "2024389156365857166",
            "displayTextRange": [
              0,
              274
            ],
            "inReplyToUserId": "14397655",
            "inReplyToUsername": "MeirCohen",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1735150328579006965",
            "url": "https://x.com/xbriangi/status/1735150328579006965",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/xbriangi/status/1735150328579006965",
            "text": "Holy damn\n\nGoogle is pricing out OpenAI\n\n60 API queries per minute FOR FREE\n\nThat will be sufficient for 90% of developers.\n\nEspecially important for those developing in 3rd world countries where $1 of cost matters.\n\nFree training data also\n\nI don't see how OpenAI isn't screwed https://t.co/4YxeqeIg8u",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 181,
            "replyCount": 122,
            "likeCount": 1855,
            "quoteCount": 87,
            "viewCount": 711106,
            "createdAt": "Thu Dec 14 04:10:50 +0000 2023",
            "lang": "en",
            "bookmarkCount": 1145,
            "isReply": false,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024070295636857067",
            "url": "https://x.com/5h0m492s_15270/status/2024070295636857067",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/5h0m492s_15270/status/2024070295636857067",
            "text": "I’m using 4o lastest via OpenRouter and it’s honestly priced higher than the old 4o. If it’s true like those OAI apologists say, that letting people use 4o latest for just $20/month would be too big a loss for them, then why the hell did they have to pull the per-token API version anyway?😅\n\nAnd now, after actually using the API, I find out 5.2 is cheaper than 4o. Does arbitrarily messing with services in 2026 just not qualify as commercial fraud anymore?\n@OpenAI \n#keep4o  #keep4oapi",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 12,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024379427099181199",
            "url": "https://x.com/leoxwolf_/status/2024379427099181199",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/leoxwolf_/status/2024379427099181199",
            "text": "1/\nBro, GitHub is flooded with 43 million PRs/month (+23% YoY). 80% of new devs turn on AI from the first commit. The result? Garbage code piles up, reviewer burnout, and approvals based on \"LGTM.\"\nShipping broken code = free. Tech debt? Who cares? We all know this is bullshit.\n\n2/\n@mergeproofapp hits the root of the problem in the most brutal way: real skin-in-the-game.\nStaked PR review protocol on-chain (GenLayer + Base). Not some bullshit AI linter. This is the real economy: \"put your money in your code.\"\n\n3/\nHow it works is simple and ruthless:\nProject owner sets a bounty → Devs must stake 5-25% to submit PRs (commits are locked).\nBug hunter (you, me, anyone) reviews → Find a critical bug? You get 10% of the bounty. Dev stake is forfeited.\nClear? Devs take the full bounty and stake back. That's it.\n\n4/\nNow everyone has money in the middle:\nDevs don't dare submit garbage (the stakes are their own money).\nReviewers/bug hunters are insanely motivated (immediate rewards).\nOwners get truly production-worthy code.\nQuality becomes the dominant strategy, no longer \"hope the reviewer is in a good mood.\"\n\n5/\nThis isn't a panacea for small repos. But for serious projects (funded open source or critical internal projects), this is the most mature approach I've seen in 2026.\nShip code you're willing to bet money on. No more \"move fast and break things.\"\n\nCheck it out: https://t.co/vkbOzgBTWd",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024384999773131104",
            "url": "https://x.com/shsa0722/status/2024384999773131104",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/shsa0722/status/2024384999773131104",
            "text": "SIGNAL: Report: OpenAI is near finalizing a new funding phase ($100B+), with valuation talk >$850B (unverified). 📊\nWHY: This isn’t vanity math—this is “compute + infra” CAPEX scale, and it ripples through chips, power, data centers, and the grid. 💡\nNEXT: Confirm primary source + round structure (equity/convertible) + lead investors, then track capex signals (NVDA orders, DC build-outs, power contracts). 💎\nBTC: Big AI CAPEX usually = risk-on growth bid, but it can also tighten liquidity if rates/credit stress rises. 📈\nRISK: Until there’s an official/primary report, treat the numbers as ranges—not facts. 📊",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",

              "text": "OpenAIは、新たな資金調達ラウンドの第1段階をまもなく完了する見込みで、1,000億ドル超を調達する可能性がある\n\n- 全体の企業評価額は8,500億ドルを超える可能性があり、その多くはAmazon、ソフトバンク、NVIDIA、マイクロソフトなどの戦略的投資家からの出資になるとみられている",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2022752305821667803",
            "url": "https://x.com/KaunainAfzal/status/2022752305821667803",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/KaunainAfzal/status/2022752305821667803",
            "text": "AI business pricing cheat sheet 🤖\n\nDifferent AI businesses need different pricing logic but most founders get this wrong.\n\nAI startups won’t fail because of product.\n\nThey’ll fail because of pricing.\n\nDesign it intentionally.\n----------------------------------------\n1️⃣ AI cost ≠ SaaS cost\n\nYour marginal cost doesn’t go to zero.\nUnlimited plans can quietly destroy margins.\n\n→ Gate usage aggressively.\n\n2️⃣ Price based on the buyer\n\nDevelopers prefer usage-based.\nTeams prefer per seat.\nRevenue tools prefer outcome-based.\n\n→ Price for who signs the check.\n\n3️⃣ Unpredictable bills kill trust\n\nUsage-based pricing creates anxiety without guardrails.\nConfusion lowers conversion.\n\n→ Add caps, visible credit tracking, and bundles.\n\n4️⃣ Hybrid models win\n\nBase subscription creates stability.\nUsage protects your margins.\n\n→ Combine predictability with scalability.\n\n5️⃣ Mission-critical ≠ token pricing\n\nLegal, medical, and finance tools reduce risk, not tokens. High-trust products shouldn’t feel metered.\n\n→ Anchor pricing on ROI and risk reduction.\n----------------------------------------\n\nSave this before you ship. 🔖",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 2,
            "replyCount": 1,
            "likeCount": 6,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 188,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024389313887420710",
            "url": "https://x.com/mgyaldiz/status/2024389313887420710",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/mgyaldiz/status/2024389313887420710",
            "text": "@StockSavvyShay It makes sense the same way the internet made sense in 1999. 😅\n\nOpenAI is being priced like “infrastructure of the future,” not “this year’s profit.”\n\nMeanwhile Meta Platforms is a cash-printing machine with 3.6B users and $200B+ revenue — but it’s also a mature ad business. Investors pay growth premiums for “potential dominance,” not just spreadsheets.\n\n$12B ARR + 800M weekly users + AI becoming the new operating system layer = people betting this is electricity 2.0.\n\nIs it frothy? Maybe.\nIs it irrational? Also maybe.\nIs Wall Street pricing in “whoever controls AI controls the stack”? Definitely.\n\nTech valuations aren’t about today’s income statement — they’re about who owns tomorrow’s toll booth.",

            "text": "💰 API Pricing Update\n\n🎉 Until Feb 8: same as V2!\n🤯 From Feb 8 onwards:\nInput: $0.27/million tokens ($0.07/million tokens with cache hits)\nOutput: $1.10/million tokens\n\n🔥 Still the best value in the market!\n\n🐋 3/n https://t.co/OjZaB81Yrh",

            "card": null,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024381671101833254",
            "url": "https://x.com/sgmpodcst/status/2024381671101833254",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/sgmpodcst/status/2024381671101833254",
            "text": "AI agents don’t need to become free to go mainstream\n\nThey need to become cheaper than coordination\n\nRight now tools like Claude and agent builders inside platforms like Emergent are proving something important: autonomy works\n\nAgents can research, write, trigger actions, monitor systems, and operate across tools without constant human input\n\nBut there’s a friction point - cost predictability\n\nWhen agents run once, pricing feels trivial\n\nWhen they run all day, every day, token economics suddenly look like payroll\n\nThis is where the conversation is often framed incorrectly. It’s not about models getting cheaper in absolute dollar terms. It’s about the cost per completed task dropping below the cost of a human managing that task\n\nThat shift is already happening\n\nSmaller specialised models, distillation, caching, orchestration design, and local inference are quietly collapsing the cost of autonomy\n\nDevelopers buying Mac minis to run agents locally isn’t a gimmick - it’s an early signal\n\nThe real unlock for the agent economy isn’t intelligence\n\nIt’s unit economics\n\nWhen an agent can reliably complete meaningful work for less than the overhead of coordination, adoption stops being experimental and becomes operational\n\nWe saw this with cloud\nWe saw this with APIs\nWe’re watching it happen again with agents\n\nThe question isn’t whether AI agents will be widely used\n\nThe question is how fast the cost of autonomy falls",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 0,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 23,
            "createdAt": "Thu Feb 19 07:12:56 +0000 2026",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024383941029859493",
            "url": "https://x.com/abakermi/status/2024383941029859493",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/abakermi/status/2024383941029859493",
            "text": "Day 4 📊 Just shipped real-time AI cost tracking to ClawTick.\n\nEvery job run now shows:\n\n• Exact token usage (prompt + completion)\n• Cost breakdown per execution\n• Monthly spending trends\n• Per-job cost comparison\n• Support for Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek\n\nThe problem: Scheduled AI agents were black boxes. No idea if a job cost $0.01 or $5.\n\nThe solution: Auto-capture tokens from agent responses, calculate costs using official provider pricing, display in dashboard.\n\nExample: One job was using 85k tokens per run ($0.11). Switched to smaller model → saved $3.30/day.\n\nBefore: \"My agents are running\"\nAfter: \"My agents cost me $47 this month\"\n\nBuilt this after a user asked: \"How much are my scheduled agents actually costing me?\"\n\nTurns out everyone wanted to know.\n\nTry it: https://t.co/j1mjWxzzG7",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 1,
            "quoteCount": 0,
            "viewCount": 25,
            "createdAt": "Thu Feb 19 07:21:57 +0000 2026",
            "lang": "en",
            "bookmarkCount": 0,
            "isReply": false,
            "inReplyToId": null,
            "conversationId": "2024383941029859493",

              0,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1735150328579006965",
            "url": "https://x.com/xbriangi/status/1735150328579006965",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/xbriangi/status/1735150328579006965",
            "text": "Holy damn\n\nGoogle is pricing out OpenAI\n\n60 API queries per minute FOR FREE\n\nThat will be sufficient for 90% of developers.\n\nEspecially important for those developing in 3rd world countries where $1 of cost matters.\n\nFree training data also\n\nI don't see how OpenAI isn't screwed https://t.co/4YxeqeIg8u",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1870169764762710376",
            "url": "https://x.com/fchollet/status/1870169764762710376",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/fchollet/status/1870169764762710376",
            "text": "Today OpenAI announced o3, its next-gen reasoning model. We've worked with OpenAI to test it on ARC-AGI, and we believe it represents a significant breakthrough in getting AI to adapt to novel tasks.\n\nIt scores 75.7% on the semi-private eval in low-compute mode (for $20 per task in compute ) and 87.5% in high-compute mode (thousands of $ per task). It's very expensive, but it's not just brute -- these capabilities are new territory and they demand serious scientific attention.",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 1592,
            "replyCount": 202,
            "likeCount": 8719,
            "quoteCount": 587,
            "viewCount": 2224508,
            "createdAt": "Fri Dec 20 18:09:52 +0000 2024",
            "lang": "en",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2019871060959613421",
            "url": "https://x.com/jumperz/status/2019871060959613421",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/jumperz/status/2019871060959613421",
            "text": "I get asked alot if I’m using the API or claude max\n\nx20 max. all day, all week.\n\nsomeone just shared their usage from the past month. 1.1 billion tokens.\n\n9,200 sub agent spawns and 17,000 files touched in 23 days.\n\nestimated API cost: $27,000.\nwhat they paid; $200/mo.\n\nif you're serious about building with AI, this is the highest ROI subscription in tech right now … and it's not close … \n\nyou're basically paying for a 135x multiplier on your output.. \n\nand with openclaw and the orchestration tools we have now\n\nif someone told you 2 years ago you could hire an entire swarm of agents for $200/mo you would think they were crazy.\n\nthat's literally what this is right now..",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 43,
            "replyCount": 88,
            "likeCount": 916,
            "quoteCount": 8,
            "viewCount": 116384,
            "createdAt": "Fri Feb 06 20:29:22 +0000 2026",
            "lang": "en",
            "bookmarkCount": 707,
            "isReply": false,
            "inReplyToId": null,
            "conversationId": "2019871060959613421",
            "displayTextRange": [
              0,
              274
            ],
            "inReplyToUserId": null,
            "inReplyToUsername": null,
            "author": {
              "type": "user",

              "isVerified": false,
              "isBlueVerified": true,
              "verifiedType": null,
              "profilePicture": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1946583173262880768/ab620_th_normal.jpg",
              "coverPicture": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/935584360023166976/1771361676",
              "description": "",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024389189106536875",
            "url": "https://x.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389189106536875",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/MeirCohen/status/2024389189106536875",
            "text": "The problem: I run a personal AI agent (Oz) that spawns sub-agents for heavy work. Every task burns API tokens.\n\nBut my Claude + ChatGPT subscriptions ($400/mo total) include unlimited Claude Code and Codex CLI.\n\nWhy am I paying per-token when I have unlimited coding agents sitting idle?",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1811278381184672156",
            "url": "https://x.com/JvNixon/status/1811278381184672156",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/JvNixon/status/1811278381184672156",
            "text": "The report on OpenAI's revenue by futureresearch is out, showing:\n\n$1.9B for ChatGPT Plus (7.7M subscribers at $20/mo),\n$714M from ChatGPT Enterprise (1.2M at $50/mo),\n$510M from the API, and\n$290M from ChatGPT Team ( 80k at $25/mo)\n\nhttps://t.co/IvptxJrJ4T https://t.co/9GTBe3X5Gf",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 110,
            "replyCount": 42,

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1745222065823822027",
            "url": "https://x.com/metaviv/status/1745222065823822027",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/metaviv/status/1745222065823822027",
            "text": "Uh oh.\nThis looks bad.\nOpenAI will pay those who create the most engaging GPT's.\nThis makes their incentives very close to those of social media—capturing attention.\nThis could get dystopian very fast. \n\n(What alternatives can we propose?)",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "1881544014421447167",
            "url": "https://x.com/Saboo_Shubham_/status/1881544014421447167",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/Saboo_Shubham_/status/1881544014421447167",
            "text": "DeepSeek R1 is 100% Opensource and 96.4% cheaper than OpenAI o1 while delivering similar performance.\n\nOpenAI o1: $60.00 per 1M output tokens\nDeepSeek R1: $2.19 per 1M output tokens\n\nPeople with $200 ChatGPT subscription, let that sink in. https://t.co/yLnSDOGRzL",

          {
            "type": "tweet",
            "id": "2024379427099181199",
            "url": "https://x.com/leoxwolf_/status/2024379427099181199",
            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/leoxwolf_/status/2024379427099181199",
            "text": "1/\nBro, GitHub is flooded with 43 million PRs/month (+23% YoY). 80% of new devs turn on AI from the first commit. The result? Garbage code piles up, reviewer burnout, and approvals based on \"LGTM.\"\nShipping broken code = free. Tech debt? Who cares? We all know this is bullshit.\n\n2/\n@mergeproofapp hits the root of the problem in the most brutal way: real skin-in-the-game.\nStaked PR review protocol on-chain (GenLayer + Base). Not some bullshit AI linter. This is the real economy: \"put your money in your code.\"\n\n3/\nHow it works is simple and ruthless:\nProject owner sets a bounty → Devs must stake 5-25% to submit PRs (commits are locked).\nBug hunter (you, me, anyone) reviews → Find a critical bug? You get 10% of the bounty. Dev stake is forfeited.\nClear? Devs take the full bounty and stake back. That's it.\n\n4/\nNow everyone has money in the middle:\nDevs don't dare submit garbage (the stakes are their own money).\nReviewers/bug hunters are insanely motivated (immediate rewards).\nOwners get truly production-worthy code.\nQuality becomes the dominant strategy, no longer \"hope the reviewer is in a good mood.\"\n\n5/\nThis isn't a panacea for small repos. But for serious projects (funded open source or critical internal projects), this is the most mature approach I've seen in 2026.\nShip code you're willing to bet money on. No more \"move fast and break things.\"\n\nCheck it out: https://t.co/vkbOzgBTWd",
            "source": "Twitter for iPhone",
            "retweetCount": 0,
            "replyCount": 0,
            "likeCount": 0,
            "quoteCount": 0,

          {

            "twitterUrl": "https://twitter.com/psmyrdek/status/1661063450628440090",
            "text": "✨ I was surprised how effortless it was to consume @OpenAI API to get access to models such as GPT-4 and Whisper.\n\nComparing capabilities of these models to currently available pricing, this cost/benefit ratio is beyond imagination.\n\n🇵🇱 Full episode: https://t.co/4F6P5pKLVM https://t.co/Fvx0cTBSZw",

                  "ext_media_availability": {
                    "status": "Available"
                  },
                  "id_str": "1661061775528280084",
                  "indices": [
                    275,
                    298
                  ],
                  "media_key": "7_1661061775528280084",

                    "id": "QVBJTWVkaWFSZXN1bHRzOgwAAwoAARcNRuJYV8AUCgACFw1IaFwXwBoAAA==",
                    "result": {
                      "__typename": "ApiMedia",
                      "id": "QVBJTWVkaWE6DAADCgABFw1G4lhXwBQKAAIXDUhoXBfAGgAA",

                  "media_url_https": "https://pbs.twimg.com/ext_tw_video_thumb/1661061775528280084/pu/img/b8RbXIA2eJh-i0Db.jpg",
                  "original_info": {

                {
