    "5d0fb29863b07f569c1b8a7d383b62f4c80cef5ceb6d5102822ed381fb2d4a58": {
      "expiresAtEpochMs": 1771491732304,
      "value": {
        "code": 200,
        "message": "OK",
        "tweet": {
          "url": "https://x.com/nickscamara_/status/2024226351369376211",
          "id": "2024226351369376211",
          "text": "",
          "raw_text": {
            "text": "https://t.co/bdyDeJgRrg",
            "facets": []
          },
          "author": {
            "id": "1058802033757691905",
            "name": "Nicolas Camara",
            "screen_name": "nickscamara_",
            "avatar_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1957959002568159232/LbXLiTBS_200x200.jpg",
            "banner_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/1058802033757691905/1755630486",
            "description": "co-founder & cto @firecrawl 🔥 / yc s22 / brazilian 🇧🇷 prev built mendable (AI chat used by coinbase, snapchat)",
            "location": "San Francisco",
            "url": "https://x.com/nickscamara_",
            "followers": 10788,
            "following": 373,
            "joined": "Sat Nov 03 19:24:11 +0000 2018",
            "likes": 4490,
            "media_count": 286,
            "protected": false,
            "website": {
              "url": "https://firecrawl.dev",
              "display_url": "firecrawl.dev"
            },
            "tweets": 2936,
            "avatar_color": null
          },
          "replies": 6,
          "retweets": 7,
          "likes": 70,
          "bookmarks": 155,
          "created_at": "Wed Feb 18 20:55:45 +0000 2026",
          "created_timestamp": 1771448145,
          "possibly_sensitive": false,
          "views": 29499,
          "is_note_tweet": false,
          "community_note": null,
          "lang": null,
          "replying_to": null,
          "replying_to_status": null,
          "source": "Twitter Web App",
          "article": {
            "created_at": "2026-02-18T20:55:45.000Z",
            "modified_at": "2026-02-18T20:55:45.000Z",
            "id": "2024194854893629440",
            "title": "Your browser is the bottleneck for OpenClaw",
            "preview_text": "Everyone in the valley has either tried OpenClaw or has a coworker shipping something with it. And one of the first problems people run into is browser automation.\nThe default setup is to let OpenClaw",
            "cover_media": {
              "id": "QVBJTWVkaWE6DAAFCgABHBd4vzXbUAEKAAIOsZ7XBZagAQAA",
              "media_id": "2024219320163979265",
              "media_info": {
                "__typename": "ApiImage",
                "color_info": {
                  "palette": [
                    {
                      "percentage": 52.05,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 240,
                        "green": 243,
                        "red": 248
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 36.72,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 88,
                        "green": 137,
                        "red": 250
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 4.86,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 158,
                        "green": 185,
                        "red": 249
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 3.48,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 6,
                        "green": 7,
                        "red": 10
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 0.47,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 30,
                        "green": 51,
                        "red": 102
                      }
                    }
                  ]
                },
                "original_img_height": 800,
                "original_img_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HBd4vzXbUAEyUEQ.jpg",
                "original_img_width": 2000
              },
              "media_key": "3_2024219320163979265"
            },
            "content": {
              "blocks": [
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5j6hf",
                  "text": "Everyone in the valley has either tried OpenClaw or has a coworker shipping something with it. And one of the first problems people run into is browser automation.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 18,
                      "offset": 43,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 21,
                      "offset": 212,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "dd355",
                  "text": "The default setup is to let OpenClaw drive your local browser. It works for a few workflows, but the costs show up quickly: you’re putting an agent in the same environment as your real browsing state, enabling a massive security flaw and the moment you try to run a few sessions in parallel your machine becomes the bottleneck; RAM spikes, the agent slows down, runs get flaky.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "d8d1e",
                  "text": "We built Browser Sandbox in Firecrawl because we kept hitting that same ceiling: local browsers behave like dev tooling, not infrastructure. ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 7,
                      "offset": 39,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 38,
                      "offset": 47,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "9ifg4",
                  "text": "Browser Sandbox moves that work into a secure, remote, disposable browser environment. No local Chromium installs. No driver setup. agent-browser and Playwright are already there. Your agent can spin up a browser on demand, one session or dozens, without tying the workload to the machine it’s running on.  Your OpenClaw agent can run on a free-tier EC2 instance, a Raspberry Pi, or whatever you’ve got, while the browsing happens elsewhere.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "7und9",
                  "text": "I encourage you to try it out.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "c3tbp",
                  "text": "",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "54mon",
                  "text": "Setting up Firecrawl",
                  "type": "header-one"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "ahhfs",
                  "text": "Tell your agent to install Firecrawl with 1 command:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5uv2u",
                  "text": "npx -y firecrawl-cli init --browser",
                  "type": "blockquote"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 0,
                      "length": 13,
                      "offset": 18
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "1snqf",
                  "text": "This installs the Firecrawl CLI, pops a browser so you can authenticate in Firecrawl and then installs the skill.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 43,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "fj6n0",
                  "text": "Your agent is now ready to browse the web. Once installed have your OpenClaw agent try it. ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "c6c94",
                  "text": "Tell your agent:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "funk6",
                  "text": "“Use Firecrawl Browser Sandbox to open Hacker News and get me the top 5 news of the day and the first 10 comments on each”",
                  "type": "blockquote"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 13,
                      "offset": 55,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 12,
                      "offset": 161,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "39qnd",
                  "text": "Under the hood, the firecrawl browser ... commands use agent-browser interface to execute commands in a secure sandbox. That matters because your agent can issu intent-level commands (“open”, “click”, “fill”, “snapshot”, “scrape”) instead of generating and debugging Playwright code. Playwright is still there if you need it.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "7euq0",
                  "text": "What your agent will end up doing looks like this:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {
                    "urls": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 24,
                        "text": "https://news.ycombinator.com",
                        "toIndex": 52
                      }
                    ]
                  },
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "l502",
                  "text": "firecrawl browser \"open https://news.ycombinator.com\"\nfirecrawl browser \"snapshot\"\nfirecrawl browser \"scrape\"\nfirecrawl browser close",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "56rrk",
                  "text": "A few mechanics worth calling out:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 23,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "93k20",
                  "text": "Shorthand auto-session: the shorthand form (firecrawl browser \"...\") will auto-launch a sandbox session if there isn’t one active, so the agent doesn’t need to manage session lifecycle up front.",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 25,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "226r2",
                  "text": "agent-browser by default: those quoted commands are sent to agent-browser automatically inside the sandbox.",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 38,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "9lmc5",
                  "text": "Context offloading + Token efficiency: the agent gets back artifacts (snapshot/extracted content) instead of hauling raw DOM/driver logs into the prompt. It also uses the file system to saved fetched pages and interactions, only querying it when needed.",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 4,
                      "offset": 40,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 12,
                      "offset": 45,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "7blcc",
                  "text": "And the best part is you get a reliable full web toolkit. Scrape, search, and browser automation all through a single CLI that your agent already knows how to use.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                }
              ],
              "entityMap": [
                {
                  "key": "0",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "url": "https://github.com/firecrawl/cli"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Mutable",
                    "type": "LINK"
                  }
                }
              ]
            },
            "media_entities": []
          },
          "twitter_card": "tweet",
          "color": null,
          "provider": "twitter"
        }
      }
    },

    "017b11c03826dbe8e6cc75a9d2d934288b53dba2f5437c6983d1be69cc82e07c": {
      "expiresAtEpochMs": 1771491740060,
      "value": {
        "code": 200,
        "message": "OK",
        "tweet": {
          "url": "https://x.com/Legendaryy/status/2022270816679772598",
          "id": "2022270816679772598",
          "text": "",
          "raw_text": {
            "text": "https://t.co/OHOPI6xsEO",
            "facets": []
          },
          "author": {
            "id": "574598857",
            "name": "Legendary",
            "screen_name": "Legendaryy",
            "avatar_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1931964302065356800/uVnG_yqH_200x200.jpg",
            "banner_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/574598857/1764251763",
            "description": "life is good | building the @modernmarket_",
            "location": "",
            "url": "https://x.com/Legendaryy",
            "followers": 54930,
            "following": 8520,
            "joined": "Tue May 08 16:17:28 +0000 2012",
            "likes": 47453,
            "media_count": 5336,
            "protected": false,
            "website": null,
            "tweets": 58214,
            "avatar_color": null
          },
          "replies": 18,
          "retweets": 29,
          "likes": 349,
          "bookmarks": 726,
          "created_at": "Fri Feb 13 11:25:09 +0000 2026",
          "created_timestamp": 1770981909,
          "possibly_sensitive": false,
          "views": 54492,
          "is_note_tweet": false,
          "community_note": null,
          "lang": null,
          "replying_to": null,
          "replying_to_status": null,
          "source": "Twitter Web App",
          "article": {
            "created_at": "2026-02-13T11:25:09.000Z",
            "modified_at": "2026-02-13T11:25:09.000Z",
            "id": "2022264799845826560",
            "title": "Your Agent Is Only as Good as Its Search",
            "preview_text": "Brave just dropped the LLM Context API. Tavily ships agent skills. Exa hit sub-200ms. Perplexity Sonar does multi-step research. Five search APIs, five different philosophies. Here's which one to use",
            "cover_media": {
              "id": "QVBJTWVkaWE6DAAFCgABHBCMgwIXwAAKAAIAAAAAIj+uyQAA",
              "media_id": "2022270726993068032",
              "media_info": {
                "__typename": "ApiImage",
                "color_info": {
                  "palette": [
                    {
                      "percentage": 83.36,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 18,
                        "green": 17,
                        "red": 15
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 10.42,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 99,
                        "green": 114,
                        "red": 60
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 3.6,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 37,
                        "green": 59,
                        "red": 103
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 0.72,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 50,
                        "green": 86,
                        "red": 170
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 0.63,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 154,
                        "green": 174,
                        "red": 95
                      }
                    }
                  ]
                },
                "original_img_height": 1306,
                "original_img_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HBCMgwIXwAAmCYM.jpg",
                "original_img_width": 3264
              },
              "media_key": "3_2022270726993068032"
            },
            "content": {
              "blocks": [
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "9q7v2",
                  "text": "Brave just dropped the LLM Context API. Tavily ships agent skills. Exa hit sub-200ms. Perplexity Sonar does multi-step research. Five search APIs, five different philosophies. Here's which one to use for what.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "6bvss",
                  "text": "Search is the bottleneck nobody talks about",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "8c0bp",
                  "text": "Everyone's debating which LLM to use for their agent. Claude vs GPT vs Gemini vs the new open-weights models. But there's a more fundamental question most builders skip: where does your agent get its information?",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "95fl5",
                  "text": "An agent that can't search well can't reason well. It doesn't matter if you're running Opus 4.6 or M2.5 if the search results feeding your model are stale, shallow, or poorly formatted. The model hallucinates. The agent loops. The user gets garbage.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "1s26d",
                  "text": "Microsoft killed the Bing Search API in August 2025. That left a gap. Brave, Tavily, Exa, Perplexity, and Firecrawl all rushed in with different answers to the same question: how do you get the web into an LLM efficiently?",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "3dps9",
                  "text": "The timing of Brave's announcement is worth noting. They dropped the LLM Context API on February 12, the same day MiniMax released M2.5. When open-weight models hit frontier performance at $0.15/task, the quality of search context feeding those models becomes the actual differentiator. Brave's own research backs this up: they showed that a weaker open-weight model (Qwen3) with high-quality search context outperformed ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Mode.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "42r8c",
                  "text": "In a head-to-head evaluation of 1,500 queries, Ask Brave (powered by Qwen3 + LLM Context API) scored a 4.66/5 absolute category rating, beating ChatGPT (4.32), Google AI Mode (4.39), and Perplexity (4.01). Only Grok (4.71) scored higher. Their conclusion: context quality matters more than model capability.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "en1fq",
                  "text": "Five APIs, five philosophies",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 0,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "9gdmm",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 21,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "b7uv0",
                  "text": "Brave LLM Context API",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {
                    "mentions": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 51,
                        "text": "brave",
                        "toIndex": 57
                      }
                    ]
                  },
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "84mqk",
                  "text": "This is the big one that triggered this breakdown. @brave isn't wrapping Google or Bing. They own an independent search index of 35+ billion pages, refreshed with 100+ million daily changes. The new LLM Context endpoint goes beyond standard web results. It performs a full search, then extracts \"smart chunks\" from each page in real-time: clean text, structured data, JSON-LD schemas, table extraction, code context, forum discussions, YouTube captions. These chunks get ranked by relevance and compiled into a token-efficient format optimized for LLM consumption.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "a3fdp",
                  "text": "Latency: under 600ms at p90 for the full pipeline (search + extraction + ranking), with less than 130ms overhead on top of normal search. You can set a token budget to control costs at the request level. Goggles support lets you filter, boost, or downrank results by domain. SOC 2 Type II attested. Zero Data Retention. No conflicts of interest: they don't train their own LLMs on your queries.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 1,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "60ehg",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 6,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "6v0qt",
                  "text": "Tavily",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {
                    "mentions": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 0,
                        "text": "tavilyai",
                        "toIndex": 9
                      }
                    ]
                  },
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "f8218",
                  "text": "@tavilyai has become the default search API in the LangChain/LlamaIndex ecosystem, used by 800,000+ developers. It's purpose-built for AI agents with four endpoints: Search (web discovery), Extract (pull content from URLs), Map (understand site structure), and Crawl (navigate and extract from entire sites). In January 2026 they shipped agent skills, a Vercel AI SDK integration, the /research endpoint for multi-step automated research, and domain governance controls for regulated industries.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "firbp",
                  "text": "The credit-based pricing takes some understanding. Basic search costs 1 credit, advanced costs 2. Crawling stacks mapping + extraction costs. The /research endpoint uses dynamic pricing with min/max boundaries. Credits don't roll over month to month.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 2,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "25klq",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 3,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "2ho1i",
                  "text": "Exa",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {
                    "mentions": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 0,
                        "text": "ExaAILabs",
                        "toIndex": 10
                      }
                    ]
                  },
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "3km36",
                  "text": "@ExaAILabs (formerly Metaphor) is the contrarian play. While everyone else does keyword matching or wraps Google, Exa uses neural embeddings trained on their own index of tens of billions of pages. You search by meaning, not words. Ask \"find articles explaining quantum computing like I'm five\" and it understands the intent. In October 2025 they launched Exa 2.0 with three tiers: Exa Fast (sub-350ms p50, fastest at the time), Exa Auto (default, balances latency and quality), and Exa Deep (agentic multi-search, ~3.5s p50, highest quality). Then on February 12, 2026 they shipped Exa Instant, pushing latency below 200ms for real-time applications.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "osf5",
                  "text": "The index is refreshed every minute. They built an in-house vector database to serve embeddings at low latency, trained for over a month on a 144x H200 cluster. For research agents making 50+ search calls, the 200ms savings per call from Exa Instant adds up to 10+ seconds of total savings.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 3,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "fhcj8",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 10,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "f2l70",
                  "text": "Perplexity",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5hboq",
                  "text": "Perplexity's API bundles search and LLM together. You don't just get results, you get synthesized answers with citations. The lineup: Sonar (fast, $1/M in+out tokens), Sonar Pro (deeper reasoning, $3/M input + $15/M output), Sonar Reasoning Pro (chain-of-thought with separate reasoning token costs), and Sonar Deep Research (autonomous multi-step, charges per search query made). On top of token costs, each model charges a per-request fee that varies by search context size (Low/Medium/High). The raw Search API is $5/1K requests with no token costs.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5fsj6",
                  "text": "The trade-off is clear: you give up control over the LLM layer in exchange for a complete answer pipeline. If you want to use your own model (like running M2.5 or Opus), Perplexity is the wrong choice. If you want the fastest path to grounded answers, it might be the right one. Citation tokens are now free on standard Sonar and Sonar Pro, which meaningfully reduces per-query costs. But total cost per query depends on model choice + context size + token volume, making it harder to predict than flat-rate APIs.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 4,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "fo6qm",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 9,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "ab1rt",
                  "text": "Firecrawl",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {
                    "mentions": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 0,
                        "text": "firecrawl",
                        "toIndex": 10
                      }
                    ]
                  },
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5ed9o",
                  "text": "@firecrawl is the extraction specialist. Where the others start with search, Firecrawl starts with \"give me a URL and I'll get you everything on that page.\" Their Agent handles JavaScript rendering, pagination, authentication, CAPTCHAs, and multi-page workflows automatically. Open source and self-hostable. In Firecrawl's own comparison testing, they reported 77.2% coverage and 0.638 F1 quality versus Exa's 69.2% coverage and 0.508 F1. (Note: these are vendor-published benchmarks, not independently audited.)",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "36b07",
                  "text": "It's not a search engine. It's what you use after your search engine finds the right URLs. The pricing is flat and predictable: one credit per page, always. No depth multipliers, no variable consumption. At 100K pages monthly, Firecrawl costs $83 versus Tavily's $500-800 for equivalent extraction.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 5,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "47ocr",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "52duo",
                  "text": "The comparison table",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "ce99u",
                  "text": "For the humans reading this, here is everything you need to know in one table.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 6,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "cmu6d",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "1jvo7",
                  "text": "Which API for which use case?",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "3lrkg",
                  "text": "The smart move isn't picking one. It's routing different query types to different APIs based on what each does best. Here's the routing table I'd use if I were building an agent stack today.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 7,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "3s9u9",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "9o9qt",
                  "text": "Why the Brave announcement matters specifically",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "9ujhl",
                  "text": "Brave's LLM Context API is interesting because of what it isn't. It isn't a wrapper around Google or Bing (like SerpAPI, which Google is suing). It isn't bundled with an LLM you're forced to use (like Perplexity). It isn't just a search endpoint (like their previous API).",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "2b53r",
                  "text": "What it is: a purpose-built pipeline that takes a query, searches an independent 35B-page index, extracts smart chunks from each result page in real-time, ranks those chunks for relevance, and delivers them in a token-budget-controlled format ready for any LLM. All in under 600ms.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 43,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "e7pd8",
                  "text": "Three things stand out in the announcement:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 34,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "c8qu",
                  "text": "Skills for developer environments. They open-sourced Brave Search API Skills that work in Cursor, OpenCode, ClaudeCode, and OpenClaw. This isn't an accident. The 200,000+ developers who signed up through the OpenClaw release are exactly the target audience.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 27,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "e2osm",
                  "text": "The pricing simplification. Everything under Search (Web, LLM Context, Images, News, Videos) is $5/1K requests with $5 free monthly credit. No per-token costs on the search side. Answers plan is separate at $4/1K searches + $5/M tokens. This is meaningfully simpler than Tavily's credit system or Perplexity's token + request + search stacking.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 26,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "611g",
                  "text": "The independence argument. Brave's blog makes the case that scrapers are legally risky (Google v. SerpAPI), can be shut off arbitrarily, and can't offer true Zero Data Retention since queries pass through a third party. As the only western independent search index at scale outside Big Tech, they're positioning as the safe infrastructure choice for enterprise AI.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "8u253",
                  "text": "What this means for agent builders",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 45,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "c7iht",
                  "text": "1. The search layer is becoming the real moat",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "4u4ba",
                  "text": "LLMs are commoditizing. Brave's evaluation showed an open-weight model with good search context beating frontier models with worse context. As model performance converges (M2.5 is 0.6 points from Opus), the quality of information flowing into those models becomes the primary differentiator. The search API you choose is now a product decision, not just an infrastructure one.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 33,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "4dqsg",
                  "text": "2. The hybrid approach is winning",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "dcdql",
                  "text": "The most effective agent stacks are routing different query types to different APIs. Simple factual lookups go to Brave (cheap, fast, high quality context). Research discovery goes to Exa (semantic understanding). Deep extraction goes to Firecrawl (best quality, self-hostable). Quick synthesized answers go to Sonar (no model integration needed). This multi-API approach reduces costs 40-60% compared to using one provider for everything.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 47,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "5d9mp",
                  "text": "3. Privacy and independence actually matter now",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "cdbi0",
                  "text": "Enterprise AI teams are asking where their queries go. Wrapped APIs send queries to Google or Bing. Brave's ZDR policy and SOC 2 attestation aren't just compliance checkboxes. They're competitive advantages for regulated industries where query content is sensitive. Firecrawl's open-source self-hosting option is the nuclear option for teams that need full data sovereignty.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 23,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "ckisq",
                  "text": "The OpenClaw connection",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 398,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "46uur",
                  "text": "Brave specifically mentioned the 200,000+ developers who signed up through OpenClaw. Brave Search MCP server is already integrated. For anyone running agents on OpenClaw, the LLM Context API is the path of least resistance for high-quality web grounding. But don't sleep on combining it with Exa for research tasks and Firecrawl for deep extraction. The stack is more powerful than any single tool.",
                  "type": "blockquote"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "c8jc0",
                  "text": "The search API cheat sheet",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 16,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "cp4td",
                  "text": "If you pick one: Brave LLM Context API. Independent index, LLM-optimized chunks, clean pricing, ZDR, MCP server ready. The best general-purpose search for agents in February 2026.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 16,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "248ga",
                  "text": "If you pick two: Add Exa for semantic research discovery. Brave handles factual grounding. Exa handles \"find me things like this.\" Together they cover 90% of agent search needs.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 17,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "d6snu",
                  "text": "If you go all-in: Brave for grounding + Exa for discovery + Firecrawl for extraction + Perplexity Sonar for quick answers. Route by query type. Budget $50-100/month for a comprehensive search stack that outperforms any single provider.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "fokc9",
                  "text": "Your agent is only as good as its search. The LLM handles reasoning. The search handles reality. Get both right.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                }
              ],
              "entityMap": [
                {
                  "key": "4",

    "b95739d2501277361b38950c818fa238581455dcdd406e3031dfdc346633de8f": {
      "expiresAtEpochMs": 1771491747739,
      "value": {
        "code": 200,
        "message": "OK",
        "tweet": {
          "url": "https://x.com/brave/status/2021996742456246630",
          "id": "2021996742456246630",
          "text": "New developer tools are also launching today to make the Brave Search API easier to use.\n\nFirst, we've added Skills support to Brave Search API so you can quickly enhance your AI with new capabilities. These open-source Skills can be accessed here: https://github.com/brave/brave-search-skills",
          "raw_text": {
            "text": "New developer tools are also launching today to make the Brave Search API easier to use.\n\nFirst, we've added Skills support to Brave Search API so you can quickly enhance your AI with new capabilities. These open-source Skills can be accessed here: https://t.co/ka9WkREwV8",
            "facets": [
              {
                "type": "url",
                "indices": [
                  249,
                  272
                ],
                "original": "https://t.co/ka9WkREwV8",
                "replacement": "https://github.com/brave/brave-search-skills",
                "display": "github.com/brave/brave-se…"
              }
            ]
          },
          "author": {
            "id": "257245904",

            "screen_name": "brave",
            "avatar_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1951131044171644928/7RtvNuKF_200x200.jpg",
            "banner_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/257245904/1724686389",
            "description": "Join over 109M users with our private browser, search, Web3 access & more. It only takes 60 seconds to switch. For help, contact @BraveSupport 🦁",
            "location": "San Francisco, CA",
            "url": "https://x.com/brave",
            "followers": 394913,
            "following": 182,
            "joined": "Fri Feb 25 01:18:01 +0000 2011",
            "likes": 21772,
            "media_count": 2074,
            "protected": false,
            "website": {
              "url": "https://brave.com/links/",
              "display_url": "brave.com/links/"
            },
            "tweets": 14815,

          },
          "replies": 2,
          "retweets": 14,
          "likes": 184,
          "bookmarks": 85,
          "created_at": "Thu Feb 12 17:16:04 +0000 2026",
