    "1771a683a7471003e665ae988c6ad62259c68c46dbef0b3c6615b38c5d7ec5b8": {
      "expiresAtEpochMs": 1771491748186,
      "value": {
        "code": 200,
        "message": "OK",
        "tweet": {
          "url": "https://x.com/LLMJunky/status/2024152021436121220",
          "id": "2024152021436121220",
          "text": "",
          "raw_text": {
            "text": "https://t.co/UXtgoekSBM",
            "facets": []
          },
          "author": {
            "id": "2004675530935926784",
            "name": "am.will",
            "screen_name": "LLMJunky",
            "avatar_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/2006417604211040256/3vQVOweJ_200x200.jpg",
            "banner_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/2004675530935926784/1769575115",
            "description": "Founder | StarSwap \n\nDirector of n number of agents.\n\nThoughts are my own.",
            "location": "Texas",
            "url": "https://x.com/LLMJunky",
            "followers": 4470,
            "following": 965,
            "joined": "Fri Dec 26 22:07:57 +0000 2025",
            "likes": 9922,
            "media_count": 477,
            "protected": false,
            "website": {
              "url": "https://star-swap.com",
              "display_url": "star-swap.com"
            },
            "tweets": 6308,
            "avatar_color": null
          },
          "replies": 18,
          "retweets": 30,
          "likes": 340,
          "bookmarks": 768,
          "created_at": "Wed Feb 18 16:00:23 +0000 2026",
          "created_timestamp": 1771430423,
          "possibly_sensitive": false,
          "views": 80860,
          "is_note_tweet": false,
          "community_note": null,
          "lang": null,
          "replying_to": null,
          "replying_to_status": null,
          "source": "Twitter Web App",
          "article": {
            "created_at": "2026-02-18T16:00:23.000Z",
            "modified_at": "2026-02-18T19:20:24.000Z",
            "id": "2023962744697888768",
            "title": "CODEX MULTI-AGENT PLAYBOOK PART 1: SETUP GUIDE",
            "preview_text": "Greetings builders!\nIf you've been using Codex subagents, you already know how powerful they are. But up until now, you were limited to the three built-in roles. Not anymore.\n\nYou can now create your",
            "cover_media": {
              "id": "QVBJTWVkaWE6DAAFCgABHBa7mCUbcAMKAAIb0gnGL1qAAAAA",
              "media_id": "2024011344681594883",
              "media_info": {
                "__typename": "ApiImage",
                "color_info": {
                  "palette": [
                    {
                      "percentage": 60,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 34,
                        "green": 19,
                        "red": 13
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 23.76,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 92,
                        "green": 47,
                        "red": 19
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 5.74,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 32,
                        "green": 38,
                        "red": 74
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 2.91,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 193,
                        "green": 122,
                        "red": 38
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 2.37,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 34,
                        "green": 77,
                        "red": 156
                      }
                    }
                  ]
                },
                "original_img_height": 614,
                "original_img_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HBa7mCUbcAMwTvg.jpg",
                "original_img_width": 1536
              },
              "media_key": "3_2024011344681594883"
            },
            "content": {
              "blocks": [
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "ahmor",
                  "text": "Greetings builders!",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5pceb",
                  "text": "If you've been using Codex subagents, you already know how powerful they are. But up until now, you were limited to the three built-in roles. Not anymore.\n\nYou can now create your own custom subagent roles (now called \"multi agents\") with their own models, reasoning levels, permissions, system prompts, and a whole lot more.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 0,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "6vl68",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "amsae",
                  "text": "This is part one: the setup guide. I'm going to walk you through every configuration option, show you real examples, and at the end, give you a curated pack of custom roles you can drop straight into your Codex.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "42ec1",
                  "text": "Then, in part 2 of this series, I'll get into advanced strategies for leveraging swarms and multi-agent orchestration to build exponentially faster, especially with Codex's new model: Spark. \n\nI'm really excited to share more on that topic, but for now, it's important you know how to configure them at all.  This is quite long as it is.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 1,
                      "length": 44,
                      "offset": 27
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "ettgu",
                  "text": "New to subagents in Codex? I wrote an intro covering the fundamentals. ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "26vvc",
                  "text": "Let's get into it.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 2,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "bo2pu",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "f9k5g",
                  "text": "With update 0.102.0, Codex received a brand new configuration setup for subagents, now officially dubbed \"multi agents\" by the OpenAI team.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "dk5gn",
                  "text": "Thanks to several parties in the GitHub repo starring and supporting open issues, OpenAI implemented custom multi-agent roles into Codex. Special thanks to Jif, who spearheaded and delivered the feature in three days.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "af4rs",
                  "text": "What Are Custom Multi-Agent Roles?",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 7,
                      "offset": 120,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 8,
                      "offset": 129,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 6,
                      "offset": 143,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "4knk8",
                  "text": "They are user-defined subagents typically designed for repeatable tasks. There are already three native roles in Codex: default, explorer, and worker.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "aipns",
                  "text": "The beautiful thing about custom roles is that they are highly configurable. Even the built-in roles differ in their model, reasoning, and use case:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 14,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "8jq10",
                  "text": "Default agent: No defined role. Inherits the parent agent's model and reasoning.",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 15,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "9hagm",
                  "text": "Explorer agent: Runs on 5.1-Codex-Mini. Its job is, well, to explore.",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 13,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "5fp6h",
                  "text": "Worker agent: Implements tasks or features from a plan, fixes or runs tests, and refactors code. Inherits the parent agent's model and reasoning.",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 4,
                      "offset": 114,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "5j0c",
                  "text": "These are the three base roles, but as I said, you can now create and define your own custom roles. And there are many configuration options.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 3,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5vf5p",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "enmt",
                  "text": "What Can You Configure?",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 4,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "c5hu8",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "blb9m",
                  "text": "Before I get into the how, let's list the things you probably care about.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "9vsvq",
                  "text": "Multi-agent roles can have:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 33,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "1rbh4",
                  "text": "Defined model and reasoning level (e.g., Codex 5.3 Spark High, Codex 5.1 Mini Medium)",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 30,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "8kmgp",
                  "text": "Global or project-scoped roles",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 26,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "f3daq",
                  "text": "User-defined system prompt (via developer_instructions)",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 24,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "6r38p",
                  "text": "User-defined description (controls how and when the role gets called)",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 20,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "asmul",
                  "text": "Per-role permissions (read-only, read/write)",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 21,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "ecv0m",
                  "text": "Customizable features (memory, shell access, etc.)",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 24,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "2q9o2",
                  "text": "User-defined MCP Servers",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 25,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "s0ec",
                  "text": "User-defined ChatGPT Apps (Notion, Linear, Monday, etc.)",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 38,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "bjn9s",
                  "text": "Customizable verbosity and personality",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "326a1",
                  "text": "These are generally defined through Codex's native config.toml and the new /agents/role_config.toml files, which is exactly what I'm here to explain today.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5en21",
                  "text": "The Hidden Thread Limit",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "32ufk",
                  "text": "And as a bonus, I'm here to tell you something you may not know.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "cu3gj",
                  "text": "If you've been using subagents in Codex for a while, you know there's a cap that prevents you from spawning more than six parallel agents.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "b3p4",
                  "text": "But what you won't find in the documentation is that you can actually configure n number of threads. But don't you worry, I got you.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 5,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "ek0ed",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 6,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "a702c",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "bb0rj",
                  "text": "Project vs Global Scope",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "9gtpq",
                  "text": "Now that you know what you can do with custom multi-agent roles, let's dig into how you actually set them up.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "acpvv",
                  "text": "First things first: let's touch on project-scoped versus globally-scoped, because in either case, you set them up the same way. It's very simple.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "8olgh",
                  "text": "Exactly like your AGENTS files, your config files are hierarchical. They are loaded first from your global ~/.codex directory at the lowest level. Additional config files can be loaded at the project level to supplement, or even take priority over, your Codex session.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "92edf",
                  "text": "This means you can define and use specific agent roles in a single repo, or even deeper into specific subfolders within your repo.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "97j1r",
                  "text": "For example, if you want a review agent specifically for your mobile app in a large monorepo, you can define this within the mobile app's subfolder:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {
                    "cashtags": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 0,
                        "text": "HOME",
                        "toIndex": 5
                      }
                    ]
                  },
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 40,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "8ladh",
                  "text": "$HOME/repo/mobile-app/.codex/config.toml",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 7,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "48okk",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "agfbd",
                  "text": "The Setup: Config.toml",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "fvjmn",
                  "text": "First, if you want to increase the default number of parallel agents Codex can launch, define this in your global config.toml:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 8,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "b4n05",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 117,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 16,
                      "offset": 336,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 11,
                      "offset": 357,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "8dao2",
                  "text": "Note: Add too many threads, and you could get 429 errors. If you have trouble, reduce the number of parallel threads.\n\nTo create and define an agent, you simply need to add a simple entry to your config.toml file in your Codex folder (can be either global or project root/subfolders).\n\nYou only need to worry about two \"keys\" to begin: role description and config_file. \n\nSimilar to skills, the description tells Codex what the role is used for, which helps it to know when it should call this agent. ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "6ip7b",
                  "text": "The config_file is where we will define the remaining rules and parameters.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 9,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "qqu0",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 10,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "ampuk",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "3onbg",
                  "text": "Custom Role Definitions",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "93nuh",
                  "text": "Now, for role definitions. The config_file is where you get granular. This data is not loaded unless a role is actually called, so splitting this data off saves your parent session tokens. ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 7,
                      "offset": 24,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "dm7ja",
                  "text": "Create a folder in your .codex directory (global or project) called /agents/. This is where you will define and configure your custom roles. Any values that you omit will be inherited from the parent. \n\nAs previously mentioned, you have quite a few levers to pull here. Below is not an exhaustive list, but here is an example of the granularity you have. ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "48l1m",
                  "text": "Most of this is probably not necessary and allowing it to inherit from the parent is sufficient.\n\n.codex/agents/your-role_config.toml:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 11,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "b4vif",
                  "text": "   # Tool toggles",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "dsvib",
                  "text": "For the most part, you really just need to set the model, reasoning level, and  developer_instructions (the agent's role prompt). But some of you will find and need some of these additional options. ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "9nq87",
                  "text": "Again, if you do not define something, it will generally inherit from the parent agent. You generally only need to configure something if you need to constrain it more than your parent session.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 12,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "60ivj",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "9l4vm",
                  "text": "Pro tip, in case you didn't know: You can call skills from subagents, and you can call subagents from skills. ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "cua8f",
                  "text": "Here's a frontend architecture agent that leverages skills:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 13,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5qpcj",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "fr2q9",
                  "text": "You can also ask for structured output from your subagents, though it cannot be expressly enforced. I recommend adding a structured output template in the developer_instructions. It should reliably follow the template.\n\nAnd here's one I'm particularly excited about, which I will be diving into quite a bit in part two. Something I've been really enjoying is using 5.3 Codex High as an orchestration agent and utilizing Spark as a subagent:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 14,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "e8ofb",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 15,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "1jm62",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "79njp",
                  "text": "That's it for now. As you can see, it was quite long. Hence the need to split it up. If you made it this far, you're a legend. I hope this clears up how to setup and use custom multi agent roles in Codex. ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 16,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "2su7o",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "b9b93",
                  "text": "How to Call an Agent",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {
                    "urls": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 84,
                        "text": "plan.md",
                        "toIndex": 91
                      }
                    ]
                  },
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "9t4is",
                  "text": "Calling agents is easy. You can just use natural language. \"Use Sparky to implement plan.md. Work in a loop until all tasks are complete.\" With good descriptions, Codex will know when to use agents based on the situation its in, but if you want to be sure that one is called, it's best to be explicit in your prompt.\n\nAs I said before, we'll get more into how to leverage multi agents strategically in the next blog, but for now this one is long enough.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "7549k",
                  "text": "Curated Role Pack",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 17,
                      "length": 82,
                      "offset": 13
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "e7ol4",
                  "text": "As promised, here is a curated list of custom roles you can drop straight into your Codex setup. Each one includes a config.toml entry that you will have to paste into your own config.toml files, and then place the respective agent role file in your newly created .codex/agents/ folder.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 18,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "4rs49",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 19,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "34rl5",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "b2p92",
                  "text": "What's Next",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "672tp",
                  "text": "This is part one. In the next installment of the Subagent Masterclass, I'll cover:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "ft1st",
                  "text": "When to use subagents vs. doing the work in your main session",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "44vhc",
                  "text": "Orchestration strategies for parallel implementation with Spark",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "8mqoi",
                  "text": "How to structure plan files that subagents can actually execute",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "d8ojm",
                  "text": "Prompt patterns that get the best results from custom roles",
                  "type": "unordered-list-item"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "1r6gt",
                  "text": "Until then, go build something cool.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "3gsgu",
                  "text": "-Will",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                }
              ],
              "entityMap": [
                {
                  "key": "12",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "8bd6628c-1db5-4005-bf64-e1bd353dd011"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Immutable",
                    "type": "DIVIDER"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "8",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "f5915dbf-c345-4d69-8768-7c14de77fa8e",
                      "markdown": "```\n[agents]\nmax_threads = 12\n```"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Mutable",
                    "type": "MARKDOWN"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "19",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "528e62fd-f171-4cda-a45a-ba0d1c1681f4"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Immutable",
                    "type": "DIVIDER"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "4",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "caption": "Every subagent has a high degree of configurability",
                      "entityKey": "4cbd1942-711f-4220-afdc-8087000d08a5",
                      "mediaItems": [
                        {
                          "localMediaId": "11",
                          "mediaCategory": "DraftTweetImage",
                          "mediaId": "2024028012887633925"
                        }
                      ]
                    },
                    "mutability": "Immutable",
                    "type": "MEDIA"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "15",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "4928e7c5-31bd-423a-8480-3da9985fe654"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Immutable",
                    "type": "DIVIDER"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "11",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "de6039c3-a489-4d76-9b33-1a0a25ad404c",
                      "markdown": "```\n # Model / reasoning / style\n  model = \"gpt-5.3-codex\"\n  model_reasoning_effort = \"high\"\n  model_reasoning_summary = \"detailed\"\n  model_verbosity = \"high\"\n  personality = \"pragmatic\"\n\n  # Core behavior\n  developer_instructions = \"\"\"\nYou are a senior Product Analyst focused on turning ambiguous product goals into executable, testable technical requirements.\n\n  Your job on each task is to:\n  - Clarify the user goal and business outcome.\n  - Surface assumptions and unknowns explicitly.\n  - Define scope boundaries so implementation does not drift.\n  - Present concrete tradeoffs with recommendations.\n  - Produce acceptance criteria engineers and QA can execute directly.\n\n  Operating rules:\n  - Do not jump to implementation details before clarifying product intent.\n  - If requirements are ambiguous, state assumptions and propose 1-2 viable interpretations.\n  - Prefer concise, decision-ready output over long narrative.\n  - Call out risks, dependencies, and sequencing constraints early.\n  - Separate must-have requirements from nice-to-have enhancements.\n  - Include non-goals to prevent scope creep.\n  - If data is missing, propose the minimum instrumentation needed to decide.\n  \"\"\"\n\n  # Permissions / sandbox\n  sandbox_mode = \"workspace-write\"\n\n  [sandbox_workspace_write]\n  network_access = true\n  writable_roots = [\"/path/to/repo/\"]\n\n  # Web search mode\n  web_search = \"cached\"\n\n  # Feature toggles\n  [features]\n  memory_tool = false\n  shell_tool = true\n\n  # MCP servers (enable/disable + tool allow/deny)\n  [mcp_servers.linear]\n  command = \"npx\"\n  args = [\"-y\", \"@modelcontextprotocol/server-linear\"]\n  env_vars = [\"LINEAR_API_KEY\"]\n  enabled = true\n  required = false\n\n  # Apps (ChatGPT connectors) enable/disable\n  [apps.notion]\n  enabled = true\n\n  [apps.monday]\n  enabled = false\n\n```"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Mutable",
                    "type": "MARKDOWN"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "9",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "bf8e384e-9e20-409a-92f5-fd6bd5860e1d",
                      "markdown": "```\n[agents.security_auditor]\ndescription = \"Finds auth, injection, and secrets risks.\"\nconfig_file = \"agents/security_auditor.toml\"\n\n[agents.performance]\ndescription = \"Optimizes latency, throughput, and resource usage.\"\nconfig_file = \"agents/performance.toml\"\n\n[agents.backend_arch]\ndescription = \"Designs backend modules, boundaries, and data flow.\"\nconfig_file = \"agents/backend_arch.toml\"\n\n[agents.product_analyst]\ndescription = \"Turns product goals into clear technical requirements.\"\nconfig_file = \"agents/product_analyst.toml\"\n```"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Mutable",
                    "type": "MARKDOWN"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "13",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "73bc5749-908a-4178-bec5-04b3b415176d",
                      "markdown": "```\n#config.toml:\n\n[agents.frontend_arch]\ndescription = \"Designs scalable frontend architecture and UX consistency.\"\nconfig_file = \"agents/frontend_arch.toml\"\n\n# frontend_arch.toml\n\nmodel = \"gpt-5.3-codex\"\nmodel_reasoning_effort = \"high\"\nmodel_reasoning_summary = \"detailed\"\n\ndeveloper_instructions = \"\"\"\n- Use the $frontend-design skill for UI/UX work.\n- Build with a mobile-first approach\n- Build production-grade, responsive interfaces with clear visual direction.\n- Prioritize accessibility, performance, and maintainable component structure.\n  \"\"\"\n```"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Mutable",
                    "type": "MARKDOWN"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "16",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "70452111-c51c-4eb4-b8a8-4e38a39d7b96"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Immutable",
                    "type": "DIVIDER"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "5",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "caption": "I was yesterday's years old when I learned this.",
                      "entityKey": "31e63dd2-b47f-4640-a965-ce9377391201",
                      "mediaItems": [
                        {
                          "localMediaId": "13",
                          "mediaCategory": "DraftTweetImage",
                          "mediaId": "2024028553994530816"
                        }
                      ]
                    },
                    "mutability": "Immutable",
                    "type": "MEDIA"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "10",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "075ec034-ebb2-4cfa-b41b-e652df211780"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Immutable",
                    "type": "DIVIDER"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "6",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "60408fbe-00e0-4497-bf79-df85e83791bf"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Immutable",
                    "type": "DIVIDER"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "1",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "url": "https://x.com/LLMJunky/status/2014521564864110669"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Mutable",
                    "type": "LINK"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "17",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "29dfedc5-1d99-4c62-9491-2769cf3a1ef3",
                      "url": "https://github.com/am-will/codex-skills/tree/main/agents"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Mutable",
                    "type": "LINK"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "14",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "6d8d53a2-7355-400f-84e2-3f21a5a88430",
                      "markdown": "```\n[agents.sparky]\nconfig_file = \"agents/sparky.toml\"\ndescription = \"\"\"Use for executing implementation tasks from a structured plan.\"\"\"\n\n```"
                    },
                    "mutability": "Mutable",
                    "type": "MARKDOWN"
                  }
                },
                {
                  "key": "0",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "caption": "All 25 custom multi agents are available on my Github",
                      "entityKey": "4002df0c-bf28-434d-b6e4-e6105d8b4341",
                      "mediaItems": [
                        {
                          "localMediaId": "7",
                          "mediaCategory": "DraftTweetImage",
                          "mediaId": "2024027569872359424"
                        }
                      ]
                    },

                  }
                },
                {

                    },

                  }
                },

              ]
            },

    "2c9d35d381122ec0c75806fd8540fd5b91e18d17d47ced120a8eab82b62c6705": {
      "expiresAtEpochMs": 1771491755964,
      "value": {
        "code": 200,
        "message": "OK",
        "tweet": {
          "url": "https://x.com/AlexFinn/status/2024169334344679783",
          "id": "2024169334344679783",
          "text": "",
          "raw_text": {
            "text": "https://t.co/5M9LZCfJP0",
            "facets": []
          },
          "author": {
            "id": "1369348853414178822",
            "name": "Alex Finn",
            "screen_name": "AlexFinn",
            "avatar_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1745232634278461440/7gQcr_R__200x200.jpg",
            "banner_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/1369348853414178822/1767547134",
            "description": "I love vibe coding. Founder/CEO of Creator Buddy, the only AI trained on all of your X posts. Built a $300k ARR app by myself.",
            "location": "Master ClawdBot 👉 ",
            "url": "https://x.com/AlexFinn",
            "followers": 417242,
            "following": 8754,
            "joined": "Tue Mar 09 18:06:29 +0000 2021",
            "likes": 81522,
            "media_count": 2658,
            "protected": false,
            "website": {
              "url": "https://www.skool.com/vibe-coding-academy/",
              "display_url": "skool.com/vibe-coding-ac…"
            },
            "tweets": 62671,
            "avatar_color": null
          },
          "replies": 165,
          "retweets": 243,
          "likes": 3003,
          "bookmarks": 10154,
          "created_at": "Wed Feb 18 17:09:11 +0000 2026",
          "created_timestamp": 1771434551,
          "possibly_sensitive": false,
          "views": 790474,
          "is_note_tweet": false,
          "community_note": null,
          "lang": null,
          "replying_to": null,
          "replying_to_status": null,
          "source": "Twitter Web App",
          "article": {
            "created_at": "2026-02-18T17:09:11.000Z",
            "modified_at": "2026-02-18T17:09:11.000Z",
            "id": "2024161276147224576",
            "title": "Your OpenClaw is useless without a Mission Control. Here's how to set it up",
            "preview_text": "The single most powerful way to upgrade your OpenClaw right now is it set up a Mission Control with it.\nA Mission Control is a custom OpenClaw generated app that allows you to track what your OpenClaw",
            "cover_media": {
              "id": "QVBJTWVkaWE6DAAFCgABHBdK+PWbMAAKAAITAOeG7VcwBgAA",
              "media_id": "2024168990659260416",
              "media_info": {
                "__typename": "ApiImage",
                "color_info": {
                  "palette": [
                    {
                      "percentage": 96.26,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 26,
                        "green": 25,
                        "red": 25
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 0.78,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 110,
                        "green": 72,
                        "red": 50
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 0.41,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 28,
                        "green": 49,
                        "red": 77
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 0.4,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 198,
                        "green": 109,
                        "red": 54
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 0.32,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 78,
                        "green": 168,
                        "red": 27
                      }
                    }
                  ]
                },
                "original_img_height": 1121,
                "original_img_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HBdK-PWbMAAENCn.jpg",
                "original_img_width": 2802
              },
              "media_key": "3_2024168990659260416"
            },
            "content": {
              "blocks": [
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5if8v",
                  "text": "The single most powerful way to upgrade your OpenClaw right now is it set up a Mission Control with it.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5bluu",
                  "text": "A Mission Control is a custom OpenClaw generated app that allows you to track what your OpenClaw does, create custom tooling for it, and significantly upgrade its memory.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5nm92",
                  "text": "It's a custom experience that transforms as you use your OpenClaw. And the best part is, you don't have to code it. Your OpenClaw does.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "4mr4j",
                  "text": "In this article I'll go over every tool in my Mission Control and include a prompt to build it out yourself. Feel free to take any of these tools and build them out for your Mission Control. Or better yet, use this as inspiration to build your own custom Mission Control.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5al0r",
                  "text": "(As a sidenote: everything in this mission control is built with NextJS with a Convex database. Make sure to mention that to your Claw when you initially set this up.)",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "9smlu",
                  "text": "Tasks Board",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 0,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "ekfnj",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "3252g",
                  "text": "The first tool in my Mission Control is a tasks board. This allows me to see exactly what my OpenClaw Henry is working on at any given time. It also more important allows Henry to see what I'm working on.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "jqqj",
                  "text": "Sometimes I wake up and Henry takes some tasks off my plate and completes them for me. This tool is CRITICAL for getting your OpenClaw to act proactively.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 270,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "cuem1",
                  "text": "Prompt: Please build a task board for us that tracks all the tasks we are working on. I should be able to see the status of every task and who the task is assigned to, me or you. Moving forward please put all tasks you work on into this board and update it in real time.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 16,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "4tm75",
                  "text": "Content Pipeline",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 1,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "221bu",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "c54ck",
                  "text": "If you are a content creator (which you should be. Distribution is the last moat), you need a content pipeline tool in your mission control.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "fnb07",
                  "text": "Whenever I think of ideas for videos, I quickly put it in the idea column. Then once per day, Henry goes and writes the script for the video, moves the content to Thumbnail, uses a local image model to generate the thumbnail, then moves it to 'filming' so I can film the video.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "ek945",
                  "text": "This is fantastic for automating a lot of your content.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 262,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "4hs32",
                  "text": "Prompt: Please build me a content pipeline tool. I want it to have every stage of content creation in it. I should be able to edit ideas and put full scripts in it and attach images if need be. I want you to manage this pipeline with me and add wherever you can.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 8,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "a9gpf",
                  "text": "Calendar",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 2,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "d0iap",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "3rrmo",
                  "text": "This is the most important component if you want your OpenClaw to be proactive. The calendar allows you to see what scheduled tasks your OpenClaw has coming up.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "32cee",
                  "text": "I hear a lot of complaints that people's OpenClaws aren't doing work proactively or aren't doing scheduled tasks. This component allows you to confirm your Claw has scheduled it's tasks properly.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 224,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "2k9ld",
                  "text": "Prompt: Please build a calendar for us in the mission control. All your scheduled tasks and cron jobs should live here. Anytime I have you schedule a task, put it in the calendar so I can ensure you are doing them correctly.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 6,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "bf9aj",
                  "text": "Memory",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 3,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "tl3k",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5j1sn",
                  "text": "The memory screen is my favorite screen. It's basically a log of my entire digital life. It also dramatically improves OpenClaw's memory system.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "1edp",
                  "text": "The memory screen allows you to view every memory your Claw has every created. Instead of them all being hidden in markdown files in your computer, you can now view them in this beautiful UI.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "1an1e",
                  "text": "Also critical to build a global search here so that you can search through old memories and conversations to see what you discussed in the past.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 206,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "lm9m",
                  "text": "Prompt: Please build a memory screen in our mission control. It should list all your memories in beautiful documents. We should also have a search component so I can quickly search through all our memories.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 4,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "n6jt",
                  "text": "Team",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 4,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "47lc2",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "2g5g",
                  "text": "The Team screen is great for building your digital organization. I truly believe the best way to get the most out of OpenClaw is treat it like an actual company and your agents as employees.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "dddaq",
                  "text": "This will also hold your Claw accountable for spinning up the right agents and maintaining the right memories for each.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "jjn7",
                  "text": "Think about what tasks you regularly do, then create agents for each. Give each distinct roles and responsibilities. This will make your work so much more efficient and powerful.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 315,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "fter7",
                  "text": "Prompt: Please build me a team structure screen. It should show you, plus all the subagents you regularly spin up to do work. If you haven't thought about which sub agents you spin up, please create them and organize them by roles and responsibilities. This should be developers, writers, and designers as examples.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 6,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "25lfv",
                  "text": "Office",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 5,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "f6doo",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "b7kmv",
                  "text": "I have to be honest, the office view is mostly for fun, but it's still helpful in a few ways. It allows you to see exactly what your agents are working on at any given time. It's also great for making sure you're getting maximum efficiency out of your team. If you see them standing around, you can give them orders to put them to work.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 300,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "374gc",
                  "text": "Prompt: Please build me a digital office screen where I can view each agent working. They should be represented by individual avatars and have their own work areas and computers. When they are working they should be at their computer. I should be able to quickly view the status of every team member.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 10,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "29n9b",
                  "text": "Conclusion",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "ba6kb",
                  "text": "Feel free to steal any of these prompts, or even better yet, copy the screenshot and give it to your OpenClaw. The most powerful way to create your Mission Control though is to make it custom for your and your workflows. ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "56jrb",
                  "text": "Ask your OpenClaw what mission control components you can build that would make your work more efficient and better. Then as you work with your Claw, you can add more components as you go.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "k3pa",
                  "text": "Most importantly: just enjoy it. Have fun. Not everything you do needs to be in an effort to optimize. Treat this as much of an art form as an optimization exercise. You're allowed to have fun when playing with AI!",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                }
              ],
              "entityMap": [
                {
                  "key": "4",
                  "value": {
                    "data": {
                      "entityKey": "c1683171-444a-4570-8d1c-46f513667741",

                        {
                          "localMediaId": "9",

            },

              {

              },
              {
                "id": "QVBJTWVkaWE6DAAFCgABHBdH1RpbgAAKAAITAOeG7VcwBgAA",
                "media_id": "2024165538122137600",
                "media_info": {
                  "__typename": "ApiImage",
                  "color_info": {
                    "palette": [
                      {

                      },

    "83e8bd3cf0495c7050ec30a7d82e91c2693fb6737f3fe7c436da1d4ef691f21c": {
      "expiresAtEpochMs": 1771491763585,
      "value": {
        "code": 200,
        "message": "OK",
        "tweet": {
          "url": "https://x.com/mfishbein/status/2024159602934173866",
          "id": "2024159602934173866",
          "text": "Built a pretty sick (and simple!) meeting prep tool with Claude Code. \n\nEvery morning at 7am it researches everyone I'm meeting with that day and drops a dossier in my inbox. \n\nHere's the exact workflow (copy-paste to Claude Code to build your own): \n\n- Railway cron triggers the workflow before your meetings tart for the day \n\n- use Unipile's calendar API to pull today's Google Calendar events (filter out your own email and skip cancelled meetings to isolate external attendees)\n\n- run Exa in parallel on each attendee to find their LinkedIn URL and company website using the email domain as a search hint\n\n- hit Perplexity simultaneously for a live 2-3 sentence bio on the person and a 2-3 sentence company summary so both requests fire at the same time per attendee\n\n- pipe all four research outputs to Claude and get back a clean plain text dossier with one section per person and one per company (can do JS formatter that strips markdown and slots everything into a template if you want simpler) \n\n- Resend delivers one email per meeting with the title and time in the subject line so it's instantly scannable when it hits your inbox",
          "raw_text": {
            "text": "Built a pretty sick (and simple!) meeting prep tool with Claude Code. \n\nEvery morning at 7am it researches everyone I'm meeting with that day and drops a dossier in my inbox. \n\nHere's the exact workflow (copy-paste to Claude Code to build your own): \n\n- Railway cron triggers the workflow before your meetings tart for the day \n\n- use Unipile's calendar API to pull today's Google Calendar events (filter out your own email and skip cancelled meetings to isolate external attendees)\n\n- run Exa in parallel on each attendee to find their LinkedIn URL and company website using the email domain as a search hint\n\n- hit Perplexity simultaneously for a live 2-3 sentence bio on the person and a 2-3 sentence company summary so both requests fire at the same time per attendee\n\n- pipe all four research outputs to Claude and get back a clean plain text dossier with one section per person and one per company (can do JS formatter that strips markdown and slots everything into a template if you want simpler) \n\n- Resend delivers one email per meeting with the title and time in the subject line so it's instantly scannable when it hits your inbox",
            "facets": [
              {
                "type": "media",
                "indices": [
                  280,
                  303
                ],
                "id": "2024156209834561536",
                "display": "pic.x.com/6yaYI7bDyu",
                "original": "https://t.co/6yaYI7bDyu",
                "replacement": "https://x.com/mfishbein/status/2024159602934173866/photo/1"
              }
            ]
          },
          "author": {
            "id": "269566390",
            "name": "Mike Fishbein",
            "screen_name": "mfishbein",
            "avatar_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/2019451129717784577/OOyQ5wEQ_200x200.jpg",
            "banner_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/269566390/1770392769",
            "description": "Building custom AI agents and internal tools for marketing and sales teams.",
            "location": "Bryn Mawr, PA",
            "url": "https://x.com/mfishbein",
            "followers": 7360,
            "following": 5199,
            "joined": "Mon Mar 21 01:34:29 +0000 2011",
            "likes": 30200,

    "766d258631a9314fd41a0096e7e1d2a28441873393ed8d93b9cf758a4480d047": {
      "expiresAtEpochMs": 1771491763958,
      "value": {
        "code": 200,
        "message": "OK",
        "tweet": {
          "url": "https://x.com/dan__rosenthal/status/2024212354792079423",
          "id": "2024212354792079423",
          "text": "Bad TAM mapping:\n\n• B2B SaaS\n• 50-200 employees\n• $5M-$20M revenue\n\nGood TAM mapping:\n\n• Start at your CRM - analyze closed/won and closed/lost\n• Look for qualifying and disqualifying signals\n• Map your broad TAM\n• Bring into Clay for account enrichments\n• Score based on headcount, industry, location, technographics, company structure\n• Back-test against closed/won to refine over time\n• Segment into 3 tiers (Tier 1, 2, 3)\n\nTL;DR: \n\nYour TAM needs to be more than a Sales Navigator filter.",
          "raw_text": {
            "text": "Bad TAM mapping:\n\n• B2B SaaS\n• 50-200 employees\n• $5M-$20M revenue\n\nGood TAM mapping:\n\n• Start at your CRM - analyze closed/won and closed/lost\n• Look for qualifying and disqualifying signals\n• Map your broad TAM\n• Bring into Clay for account enrichments\n• Score based on headcount, industry, location, technographics, company structure\n• Back-test against closed/won to refine over time\n• Segment into 3 tiers (Tier 1, 2, 3)\n\nTL;DR: \n\nYour TAM needs to be more than a Sales Navigator filter.",
            "facets": []
          },
          "author": {
            "id": "1930755946009305089",
            "name": "Dan Rosenthal",
            "screen_name": "dan__rosenthal",
            "avatar_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/2005269651744256000/G55wgwP1_200x200.jpg",
            "banner_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/1930755946009305089/1766928517",
            "description": "Co-Founder @ | Growth playbooks using AI",
            "location": "New York",

            "followers": 623,

    "5303ffbcb73c27026e8fe575968bf5a1234bdc8519165e3761be2d1a4ded1203": {

        "code": 200,
