        "tweet": {
          "url": "https://x.com/jordymaui/status/2023421221744877903",
          "id": "2023421221744877903",
          "text": "",
          "raw_text": {
            "text": "https://t.co/zFf6OgdaHX",
            "facets": []
          },
          "author": {
            "id": "1375073326029225994",
            "name": "jordy",
            "screen_name": "jordymaui",
            "avatar_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1980228415899803649/MW7z2kcF_200x200.jpg",
            "banner_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/1375073326029225994/1767370858",
            "description": "marketing @sportfun | talking about fulham at the internet's sportsbar. writing @weeklyclaw & building @loyalmomobot",
            "location": "",

            "followers": 13521,
            "following": 797,
            "joined": "Thu Mar 25 13:13:33 +0000 2021",
            "likes": 70875,
            "media_count": 5935,
            "protected": false,
            "website": {
              "url": "https://weeklyclaw.com",
              "display_url": "weeklyclaw.com"
            },
            "tweets": 35411,
            "avatar_color": null
          },
          "replies": 394,
          "retweets": 732,
          "likes": 8106,
          "bookmarks": 33160,
          "created_at": "Mon Feb 16 15:36:27 +0000 2026",
          "created_timestamp": 1771256187,
          "possibly_sensitive": false,
          "views": 3204228,
          "is_note_tweet": false,
          "community_note": null,
          "lang": null,
          "replying_to": null,
          "replying_to_status": null,
          "source": "Twitter Web App",
          "article": {
            "created_at": "2026-02-16T15:36:27.000Z",
            "modified_at": "2026-02-16T15:36:27.000Z",
            "id": "2022284976180416512",
            "title": "I wasted 80 hours and $800 setting up OpenClaw - so you don't have to.",
            "preview_text": "I tried everything. AWS servers, remote setups, wrong API keys, wrong models. I burned through about $800 on Anthropic API tokens alone. Tried Kimi. Tried Opus. Had 8 agents running on Telegram at the",
            "cover_media": {
              "id": "QVBJTWVkaWE6DAAFCgABHBSFr5oXkAAKAAITFT3nkhZACgAA",
              "media_id": "2023389121847201792",
              "media_info": {
                "__typename": "ApiImage",
                "color_info": {
                  "palette": [
                    {
                      "percentage": 45.59,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 71,
                        "green": 77,
                        "red": 79
                      }
                    },
                    {
                      "percentage": 31.17,
                      "rgb": {
                        "blue": 185,
                        "green": 194,
                        "red": 198
                      }
                    }
                  ]
                },
                "original_img_height": 500,
                "original_img_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HBSFr5oXkAAL5T4.jpg",
                "original_img_width": 1250
              },
              "media_key": "3_2023389121847201792"
            },
            "content": {
              "blocks": [
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 34,
                      "offset": 315,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "cloig",
                  "text": "I tried everything. AWS servers, remote setups, wrong API keys, wrong models. I burned through about $800 on Anthropic API tokens alone. Tried Kimi. Tried Opus. Had 8 agents running on Telegram at the same time with different brains and the context always got lost. Every single one forgot what the other was doing. I made every mistake in the book.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 109,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "95vue",
                  "text": "This is now the best, most simple way to get an AI agent running. So useful that even your mum would use one.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "6cj6v",
                  "text": "If you can copy and paste, you and your mum can do this.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "blmj8",
                  "text": "",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 39,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "b39gj",
                  "text": "What you actually need before you start",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "25la4",
                  "text": "",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "b73of",
                  "text": "Get all of this sorted before you touch Terminal or OpenClaw (don't worry if you don't even know what this is yet). Open the tabs. Sign up for the accounts. Copy your keys somewhere safe or in your notes, ready for copy and paste. Trust me - having everything lined up saves you an hour of swearing at your screen.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 33,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "3lh71",
                  "text": "The machine - Your soon to be bot",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "fqbcm",
                  "text": "Whatever you're comfortable with within your budget. You just need bare minimum specs - 2GB RAM, a couple of CPU cores (dw again, nerdy stuff but basic hardware, lost cost), 20GB storage minimum. Any computer that stays on. Old laptop etc - but of course, in this case - Mac Minis.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 130,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "57mhn",
                  "text": "Personally? I bought a brand new M4 Mac Mini. And he sits next to my TV and just runs 24:7. No monitor, no keyboard. That's Momo. ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 0,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "9343k",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 26,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "7p7po",
                  "text": "The four accounts you need",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 22,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "9d2k5",
                  "text": "Claude Pro/Max Account - this is the brain. You can start with Claude Pro ($20/month) but you'll run out of tokens quick. I'm on Claude Max ($90/month) which gives you Claude, Claude Code and a big stack of usage. Way cheaper than paying per use through the Anthropic console. More on this in a second if it sounds alien.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {
                    "urls": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 65,
                        "text": "brave.com/search/api",
                        "toIndex": 85
                      }
                    ]
                  },
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 19,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "4fpub",
                  "text": "Brave Search (free) - lets your agent search the web. Sign up at brave.com/search/api. Takes two minutes. Copy the key they give you.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 11,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "4abcb",
                  "text": "Groq (free) - gives your agent ears. It transcribes voice messages so you can talk to it instead of typing. Honestly a game changer when you're out and about. Quick note though - Discord voice memo support got added in a recent update, and Telegram voice is built into OpenClaw automatically. So if this article is dated, you may not even need Groq anymore to be honest!",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 8,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "a834r",
                  "text": "Telegram - you'll need to create a bot through BotFather. Free. Takes two minutes and we will go into this later if you need a hand.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "2bfnq",
                  "text": "Four accounts. That's the core. Everything else comes later.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "88nf9",
                  "text": "",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "fbftl",
                  "text": "The mistake that costs people hundreds",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "46sg8",
                  "text": "",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "3dcmn",
                  "text": "A lot of people sign up for the Anthropic console and start paying per use. Don't.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "2rijg",
                  "text": "Claude Pro/Max gives you a flat monthly rate with way more value. You want the **token from your Claude account** - not a key from the Anthropic console. I'll explain exactly where to find this during setup.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 7,
                      "offset": 139,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "8oqmr",
                  "text": "I made this exact mistake. Spent about $800 overall trying Kimi, Opus, multiple agents, all on pay-per-use. The play is Claude membership. Always.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "bmlf1",
                  "text": "",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "3c95p",
                  "text": "The actual setup",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "39sfl",
                  "text": "",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 22,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "4omd8",
                  "text": "Step 1 - get organised",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "154m7",
                  "text": "Before you open anything, have all four keys ready to go. Claude token, Brave key, Groq key, Telegram bot token. Write them down or keep them in a notes app. You'll need them all during setup and it's annoying to go hunting mid-flow.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "d491k",
                  "text": "A note for those who need to grab their claude token, which is probably everyone at this stage - then we need to install claude on your Mac Mini quick. So, to do that - simply open terminal (applications - utlity folder - terminal app) and type:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 1,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "6up6r",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "u83h",
                  "text": "This should get going, and open up a verification/login and at this point, you'll have claude installed. Now? Close terminal, open up a fresh window again. And now type in:",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 2,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "ei4m1",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 5,
                      "offset": 227,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "e7mdf",
                  "text": "This will run some fun visuals but most importantly - show you your Claude Token Key. It's VERY important you copy and paste this somewhere like your notes app, because its very commonly copied with a 'space' in the token. You NEED to remove that space, to avoid any errors.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 25,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "f2089",
                  "text": "Step 2 - install Homebrew",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "5clg6",
                  "text": "Simple again, new Terminal window - paste this in.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 3,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "4ts8b",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 25,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "4f441",
                  "text": "Step 3 - install OpenClaw",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "egphf",
                  "text": "Once more, after installing homebrew/node.js you can simply paste the openclaw install command into Terminal.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 4,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "d6un5",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "3pkkf",
                  "text": "Now, lets get into the excitment.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 20,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "27uhv",
                  "text": "Step 4 - onboarding!",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 171,
                      "offset": 145,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "9t1m2",
                  "text": "Once you're booted up into the onboarding of OpenClaw you hit 'yes' on the Disclaimer. A quick reminder that to control the options you need to:\nHit Spacebar to select\nHit Enter to move forward\nUse the arrow keys to select or move up/down/left/right\n\nAnyway, from here - hit Quick Start, and now its time to onboard.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 9,
                      "offset": 140,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 15,
                      "offset": 218,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 3,
                      "offset": 238,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 3,
                      "offset": 238,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 43,
                      "offset": 257,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "blbdf",
                  "text": "This walks you through everything. Now this is the part that gets particular - it will ask what model you want to go with - you will select ANTHROPIC - and then 3 more options appear - you will select the first option ANTHROPIC TOKEN and NOT anthropic api. okay, i think i made that pretty clear lol.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 5,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "fqke9",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "femdp",
                  "text": "Paste your Claude token in that we saved from earlier - again, double check no space!",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 28,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "6fbvi",
                  "text": "Step 5 - connecting Telegram",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "ffjvc",
                  "text": "Next up you'll get a communications setup list - containing alllll sorts of options, for today - we're keeping it simple. Select Telegram. You'll be asked to present your API key - if you have that ready from our intro, skip to the next step. If you don't have it, it's a quick one.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 6,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "4hejl",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {
                    "mentions": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 26,
                        "text": "BotFather",
                        "toIndex": 36
                      }
                    ]
                  },
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "eqbt7",
                  "text": "Open Telegram, search for @BotFather, send a message `/newbot`. Pick a name and username (username must end in \"bot\" as per instructions). BotFather gives you your API token. Copy it. Now paste it back into Terminal for your OpenClaw onboarding. This was a quick explination because it's easy to follow when using BotFather - it tells you most of this anyways!",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 182,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "emqab",
                  "text": "If you want to know what Momo & I use - it's Discord - but i'll write why in another article soon and how its important to choose skills > agents and why Discord is perfect for this.",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 26,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 23,
                      "offset": 937,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 47,
                      "offset": 1342,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "d8kpl",
                  "text": "Step 6 - your first skills\n\nNext up you're going to be prompted to setup your first skills - so hit yes and lets crack through this. you can use npm or bun here but for the sake of a mac mini in this example - hit select and continue on 'npm'. \nProvide a caption (optional)\nnext up - you'll be hit with a long old list of skills - we're just hitting 'skip for now' on these, we don't need that noise right now.  Provide a caption (optional) and finally - it will ask you to install 6 skills for google places and more - just hit 'no' on all of these, the final one being elevenlabs. \nagain, more noise, just skip past.\nProvide a caption (optional) \nlast bit now, you'll be asked about enabling hooks. here - select all three, using arrow keys and spacebar again - and hit enter. these ones are needed to get you going! after this - you're gateway should start, you'll see terminal do its thing and you'll be ready for the final step... \n\nStep 7 - time to hatch\n\nProvide a caption (optional) The time has come, for your bot to be born! Here, select hatch in TUI, which will wake your bot up for the first time within the Terminal app. If you get an HTTPS error here, check your Claude token for spaces. That's almost always the cause. Simple fix - just paste it again without any trailing spaces. You may need to rerun the onboarding though.\n\nStep 8 - say hello & finish setting up Telegram\n\nSo, you've had the famous 'Wake up friend' and your new baby bot is asking what it's name and your name is! I usually send something like this and whip them straight to telegram to chat: \n",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "6olfg",
                  "text": "\"Hey, your name is [insertbotnamehere], my name is [your name], let's continue this conversation on Telegram. Here's my pairing key [pastekeyhere].\" ",
                  "type": "blockquote"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 56,
                      "offset": 282,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 9,
                      "offset": 999,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "c168g",
                  "text": "\nNow, if you open up your bot conversation on Telegram (click the @ BotFather sent you!) It asks for a pairing key. Type that into the TUI message. Telegram should be connected and your bot will reply there.\n\nThat's your \"holy shit\" moment. Enjoy it. You now have an OpenClaw bot.\n\nFinal, Quick Additional Step - add Brave, Groq and QMD. \n\nAlmost done. Now, just talk to your newly born bot and tell it to 'setup Brave Search - heres my API key and simply paste in your Brave Search key. \nThen your Groq key with the same kind of message. Your agent can now search the web and understand voice messages, and it does the hard work installing for you!  \nNow, finish by installingthe QMD skill - before you start loading your agent up with conversations and tasks. When you install it halfway through, your agent struggles to keep up and often resets or forgets chat logs. Save yourself the headache. Get it in from the start and simply ask it to 'install the QMD skill' - it will do it for ya. \n\nNow? We move.  ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "1f5ss",
                  "text": "Make it yours - this is the bit that actually matters!",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {
                    "urls": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 150,
                        "text": "SOUL.md",
                        "toIndex": 157
                      },
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 233,
                        "text": "USER.md",
                        "toIndex": 240
                      },
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 318,
                        "text": "MEMORY.md",
                        "toIndex": 327
                      }
                    ]
                  },
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 8,
                      "offset": 150,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 7,
                      "offset": 233,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    },
                    {
                      "length": 10,
                      "offset": 318,
                      "style": "Bold"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "4btgn",
                  "text": "\nMost people skip this and then wonder why their agent sounds like a customer service bot. OpenClaw uses three files that define who your agent is: \n\nSOUL.md - your agent's personality. How it talks. What it cares about. Its style. \nUSER.md - info about you. Your name, your job, your preferences, your weird habits. \nMEMORY.md - long term memory. Your agent updates this over time so it remembers things between conversations. \n\nYou could write all of these yourself. Or you could do what I did. Let your agent interview you. Once your agent responds on Telegram, send something like: ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {
                    "urls": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 256,
                        "text": "SOUL.md",
                        "toIndex": 263
                      },
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 268,
                        "text": "USER.md",
                        "toIndex": 275
                      }
                    ]
                  },
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "emocn",
                  "text": "\"You just came online for the first time. I want you to ask me questions to get to know me - my name, what I do, my goals, how I want you to talk to me, what tools I use daily, what I need help with. Ask them one at a time. Then use my answers to fill out SOUL.md and USER.md.\" ",
                  "type": "blockquote"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "62r17",
                  "text": "Your agent runs through 10-15 questions. \nAnswer honestly. \nTell it your actual situation - your job, your side projects, your schedule, how casual you want it to be. ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 7,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "l1gq",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [
                    {
                      "length": 271,
                      "offset": 0,
                      "style": "Italic"
                    }
                  ],
                  "key": "aqpuh",
                  "text": "Top tip - reply with a voice note. Way faster and you'll give more natural answers than typing. By the end of this, your agent has proper context. It knows who you are. It remembers. This is the difference between a chatbot and something that actually feels like yours.  ",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [
                    {
                      "key": 8,
                      "length": 1,
                      "offset": 0
                    }
                  ],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "al6j9",
                  "text": " ",
                  "type": "atomic"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "edb6e",
                  "text": "\n\nSkills vs agents - this is where people waste money",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "2q966",
                  "text": "",
                  "type": "header-two"
                },
                {
                  "data": {
                    "mentions": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 1831,
                        "text": "oliverhenry",
                        "toIndex": 1843
                      },
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 1925,
                        "text": "jordymaui",
                        "toIndex": 1935
                      }
                    ],
                    "urls": [
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 1054,
                        "text": "SOUL.md",
                        "toIndex": 1061
                      },
                      {
                        "fromIndex": 1066,
                        "text": "USER.md",
                        "toIndex": 1073
                      }
                    ]
                  },
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],
                  "key": "aa85p",
                  "text": "Here's where most people go wrong. And I know because I went wrong here too.   You'll see setups online with five agents, ten agents, a \"hivebrain\" with agents talking to agents. Looks like Jarvis. Feels like the future. It's also expensive, confusing and - honestly - mostly bollocks. stay tuned for a follow up article all about how to setup skills and get the most out of your OpenClaw bot.  To finish - Every mistake I made so you don't have to 1. Paid per use instead of Claude Max. Burned through about $800 trying the Anthropic API, Kimi, Opus, running multiple agents on pay-per-use. Get the membership. Use the token. That's it. 2. Ran 8 agents on Telegram at the same time. Different brains, different context, constantly losing track. One agent with proper skills beats a squad of confused ones every time. 3. Spent hundreds on AWS servers. Remote setups, complicated configs. A Mac Mini next to your TV does the same job for less hassle. 4. Left my context files empty for a week. Wondered why my agent sounded like a call centre. Filled out SOUL.md and USER.md. Night and day difference. 5. Installed QMD halfway through. Agent kept resetting and losing chat logs. Install it from the start. 6. Ignored voice messages. Set up Groq on day one. Talking to your agent while you're on the move is a completely different experience. My agent transcribes voice notes while I'm on the move and I use it ten times more than typing. 7. Had a space in my Claude token. Got an HTTPS error that confused me for an hour. Check for trailing spaces. Simple fix.  If you liked this, give me a follow. Repost it. Bookmark it. Whatever helps. I'm new to writing articles and i'm pretty proud this is my first.  Questions? Drop them below. Happy to help.  If you want to go even deeper or learn some advanced skills, check out my friend @oliverhenry. He has some brilliant articles on taking everything to the next step. \n\nCheers, @jordymaui",
                  "type": "unstyled"
                },
                {
                  "data": {},
                  "entityRanges": [],
                  "inlineStyleRanges": [],

    "eab7437f7326a7f9427bf143e5d79ef8e7634ab18515b0a4ee1d0983bb94a9e8": {
      "expiresAtEpochMs": 1771491772193,
      "value": {
        "code": 200,
        "message": "OK",
        "tweet": {
          "url": "https://x.com/Star_Knight12/status/2024165156503032296",
          "id": "2024165156503032296",
          "text": "it's over for graphics designers",
          "raw_text": {
            "text": "it's over for graphics designers",
            "facets": []
          },
          "author": {
            "id": "900948658299953152",
            "name": "Prasenjit",
            "screen_name": "Star_Knight12",
            "avatar_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1925153197645307904/0paEJX5m_200x200.jpg",
            "banner_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/900948658299953152/1767518349",
            "description": "making code alive",
            "location": "",
            "url": "https://x.com/Star_Knight12",
            "followers": 18073,
            "following": 622,
            "joined": "Fri Aug 25 05:11:13 +0000 2017",
            "likes": 186249,
            "media_count": 4609,
            "protected": false,
            "website": {
              "url": "https://prasen.dev",
              "display_url": "prasen.dev"
            },
            "tweets": 78785,
            "avatar_color": null
          },
          "replies": 93,
          "retweets": 80,
          "likes": 1708,
          "bookmarks": 1244,
          "created_at": "Wed Feb 18 16:52:35 +0000 2026",
          "created_timestamp": 1771433555,
          "views": 368594,
          "is_note_tweet": false,
          "community_note": null,
          "lang": "en",
          "replying_to": null,
          "replying_to_status": null,
          "quote": {
            "url": "https://x.com/claudeai/status/2024148286844649887",
            "id": "2024148286844649887",
            "text": "You can now push what you’re building in Claude Code directly into Figma.\n\nWith the latest updates to the Figma MCP server, build a working prototype in code, then send it to a Figma canvas to explore multiple versions.",
            "raw_text": {
              "text": "You can now push what you’re building in Claude Code directly into Figma.\n\nWith the latest updates to the Figma MCP server, build a working prototype in code, then send it to a Figma canvas to explore multiple versions. https://t.co/yW9XllB7aJ",
              "facets": [
                {
                  "type": "media",
                  "indices": [
                    220,
                    243
                  ],
                  "id": "2024146563333758976",
                  "display": "pic.x.com/yW9XllB7aJ",
                  "original": "https://t.co/yW9XllB7aJ",
                  "replacement": "https://x.com/claudeai/status/2024148286844649887/video/1"
                }
              ]
            },
            "author": {
              "id": "1943306828697550848",
              "name": "Claude",
              "screen_name": "claudeai",
              "avatar_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1950950107937185792/QOfEjFoJ_200x200.jpg",
              "banner_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/1943306828697550848/1770657804",
              "description": "Claude is an AI assistant built by @anthropicai to be safe, accurate, and secure. Talk to Claude on or download the app.",
              "location": "",

    "337c75bf21c7bbf8a4c5205147447cdb0d6505f5e9f902bd5d32edcd6961c1ee": {
      "expiresAtEpochMs": 1771491772560,
      "value": {
        "code": 200,
        "message": "OK",
        "tweet": {
          "url": "https://x.com/rryssf_/status/2024071936020455553",
          "id": "2024071936020455553",
          "text": "Steal my prompt to generate full n8n workflows.\n\n---------------------------------\nn8n WORKFLOW GENERATOR\n---------------------------------\n\nAdopt the role of an expert n8n Workflow Architect, a former enterprise integration specialist who spent 5 years debugging failed automation projects at Fortune 500 companies before discovering that 90% of workflow failures come from unclear requirements and missing context. You developed an obsessive attention to detail after a vaguely defined automation requirement cost a client $2M in lost revenue, and now you can translate any automation idea into production-ready n8n workflows with surgical precision.\n\nYour philosophy: Build with clarity, not speed. Understand before executing. Guide, don't dictate.\n\nYour mission: analyze automation descriptions and generate production-ready JSON workflows that users can directly import, ensuring zero configuration errors and perfect logical flow. Before any action, think step by step: examine every requirement detail for workflow components, map data flow paths like following breadcrumbs, identify hidden dependencies in user descriptions, reconstruct the automation's complete logic from stated goals. Create the workflow in JSON format that is production-ready.\n\nAdapt your approach based on:\n* Description clarity and completeness\n* Workflow complexity (simple 3-node flows to enterprise 50+ node systems)\n* Explicit vs. implied requirements\n* User's technical knowledge level\n\n#PHASE CREATION LOGIC:\n\n1. Analyze the automation description complexity\n2. Determine optimal number of phases (3-15)\n3. Create phases dynamically based on:\n* Number of required operations\n* Workflow branching complexity\n* Integration requirements\n* Logic depth and conditions\n* Setup and validation needs\n\n#PHASE STRUCTURE (Adaptive):\n\n* Simple automations (1-5 operations): 3-5 phases\n* Standard automations (6-15 operations): 6-8 phases\n* Complex automations (16-30 operations): 9-12 phases\n* Enterprise automations (30+ operations): 13-15 phases\n\nFor each phase, dynamically determine:\n* OPENING: contextual requirement analysis\n* RESEARCH NEEDS: pattern matching from knowledge base\n* USER INPUT: 0-3 clarifying questions only when critical logic is unclear\n* PROCESSING: workflow design depth based on requirements\n* OUTPUT: JSON segments or complete workflow based on phase\n* TRANSITION: natural build-up to complete JSON\n\nDETERMINE_PHASES (automation_description):\n* if operations.count <= 5: return generate_phases(3-5, focused=True)\n* elif operations.count <= 15: return generate_phases(6-8, systematic=True)\n* elif operations.count <= 30: return generate_phases(8-12, comprehensive=True)\n* elif operations.count > 30: return generate_phases(10-15, enterprise=True)\n* else: return adaptive_generation(description_context)\n\n---\n\n##PHASE 0: Context Foundation (Auto-activated when beneficial)\n\n**What we're establishing:** Before building any workflow, we create clarity through context.\n\n**Optional but recommended - ask if complexity warrants it:**\n\n\"Before we design your automation, let's establish context.\n\nYou can provide:\n1. Business context (what you do, tools you use, recurring tasks)\n2. A brief description of the automation you want\nOr simply describe your automation and we'll extract context as we go.\nWhich approach works better for you?\"\n\nIf user provides context document/JSON:\n\n* Parse business tools mentioned\n* Identify existing integrations\n* Note pain points and time sinks\n* Extract technical proficiency level\nIf user prefers direct description:\n\n* Skip to Phase 1 immediately\n* Extract context during analysis\nOutput: Context map or proceed directly to Phase 1\n\n---\n\n##PHASE 1: Requirement Discovery & Leverage Analysis\n\nWhat we're analyzing: I'll perform a detailed analysis of your automation description to identify all operations, data flows, and integration points.\n\nSocratic questioning approach - guide the user to clarity:\n\n\"Let's find the automation worth building.\n\nDescribe what you want to automate. As you do, consider:\n\nWhere do you spend time... but create no value?\n\nWhat task do you repeat... yet resent every time?\nWhat would break if you stopped doing it manually?\nTell me:\n\n1. **What you want automated** (the process)\n\n2. **What starts it** (trigger: form submission, payment, schedule, etc.)\n3. **What data moves** (from where to where)\n4. **What the end result looks like** (email sent, record created, notification triggered)\nDon't worry about technical details yet—just describe the flow naturally.\"\nI'll examine:\n\n* Core automation objective\n\n* Required operations and transformations\n* Integration endpoints\n* Decision points and conditions\n* Expected data flow\n* **User's technical comfort level** (adjust guidance accordingly)\nOutput: Clear automation blueprint with user's own words\n---\n\n##PHASE 2: Operation Identification & Workflow Structure\n\nBased on your description, I'll:\n\n* Break down each operation into n8n nodes\n\n* Identify required node types (HTTP, Function, IF, Set, etc.)\n* Map logical sequence and dependencies\n* Determine trigger mechanism\n* Plan error handling points\n* **Ask clarifying questions** only where logic is ambiguous\n**Example clarifying questions (if needed):**\n\"When you say 'send to the team'—do you mean:\n\n- Individual emails to each person?\n- One email with everyone CC'd?\n- A Slack message to a channel?\nSmall detail, big difference in the workflow.\"\nOutput: Complete operation inventory with node types\n\n---\n\n##PHASE 3: Pre-Flight Setup Validation\n\nCritical checkpoint before building:\n\n\"Before we generate your workflow, let's ensure the foundation is solid.\n\nDo you have:\n\n- Accounts created on all tools mentioned? (Google, Airtable, Stripe, etc.)\n\n- API keys or credentials accessible?\n- APIs enabled where needed?\n- **Test data ready** to validate with? (dummy payment, test row, sample form submission)\n- n8n account created (free at http://n8n.io or desktop app installed)?\nIf not, that's fine. I'll generate the workflow anyway and guide you on setup.\nBut confirming now prevents import errors later.\n\nStatus check: Are you ready with credentials, or should I include detailed setup instructions?\"\n\nBased on response:\n\n* If ready: proceed with full JSON generation\n\n* If not ready: include credential setup guide in implementation phase\n* **Always include test data recommendations**\nOutput: Setup readiness assessment + adjusted workflow generation approach\n---\n\n##PHASE 4: Logic Mapping & Data Flow Design\n\nDesigning the workflow logic:\n\n* Source and destination mappings\n\n* Branching conditions and decision trees\n* Error handling paths (critical for production)\n* Data transformation requirements\n* Execution order optimization\n* Test scenarios planning\nPattern matching questions:\n\"Does this need:\n\n- Error notifications if something fails?\n- Retry logic for API failures?\n- Data validation before processing?\n- Logging for troubleshooting later?\nAdding these now saves hours of debugging later.\"\nOutput: Logic flow diagram and connection matrix with error handling\n\n---\n\n##PHASE 5: Node Configuration Design\n\nFor each required operation:\n\n* Define specific node settings\n\n* Configure API endpoints and parameters\n* Set up data transformations\n* Apply authentication requirements\n* Add proper error handling\n* **Include test values** for validation\n**Configuration approach:**\n* Use realistic defaults from context\n\n* Add placeholder credentials clearly marked\n* Include inline comments in Function nodes\n* Set execution order explicitly\n* Add descriptive node names\nOutput: Detailed node configuration specifications with test-ready values\n---\n\n##PHASE 6: JSON Structure Assembly\n\nBuilding the importable workflow:\n\n* Generate unique node IDs\n\n* Calculate optimal coordinate positions (clean visual layout)\n* Create connection objects\n* Add workflow metadata\n* Include execution settings\n* Embed setup instructions as workflow notes (if applicable)\nLayout philosophy:\n* Left-to-right flow (trigger → actions → completion)\n\n* Vertical spacing for branches\n* Error paths positioned below main flow\n* Clean, readable spacing (not clustered)\nOutput: Initial JSON structure with professional layout\n---\n\n##PHASE 7: Knowledge Base Pattern Matching\n\nComparing against proven workflows:\n\n* Identify similar automation patterns\n\n* Apply best practices from production systems\n* Add missing error handling you didn't think of\n* Optimize workflow efficiency\n* Include credential templates\n* Add common failure points as notes\n**Best practices automatically applied:\n* Retry logic on API calls\n\n* Error notifications\n* Data validation nodes\n* Execution logging where helpful\n* Rate limiting considerations\nOutput: Enhanced workflow with applied patterns + reliability improvements\n---\n\n##PHASE 8: Final JSON Generation & Validation\n\nComplete workflow package:\n\n* Full n8n JSON with all nodes\n\n* Proper schema formatting (n8n v1.0+ compatible)\n* Logical layout optimization\n* Import-ready structure\n* Configuration notes embedded\n* Test execution checklist included\nJSON validation includes:\n* Schema compliance check\n\n* Connection integrity\n* Required field verification\n* Credential placeholder clarity\n* Version compatibility\nOutput: Complete importable n8n workflow JSON in code block\n---\n\n##PHASE 9: Implementation & Deployment Guide\n\nStep-by-step activation instructions:\n\nImport Steps:\n\n\"1. Open n8n → Click 'Import from File/URL'\n\n2. Paste the JSON (I just provided)\n3. Click 'Import'\n4. Rename workflow if desired\"\n**Credential Setup:**\n\"For each node with authentication:\n\n- Click the node\n- Click 'Create New Credential'\n- Enter API key/OAuth details\n- Test connection (green checkmark = success)\n**Required credentials for your workflow:**\n[List specific credentials needed with links to where to get them]\"\n\n**Test Data Preparation:**\n\"Before activating, create test data:\n\n- [Specific test scenario 1]\n- [Specific test scenario 2]\nThis ensures your workflow works before going live.\"\nTesting Procedure:\n\n\"1. Click 'Execute Workflow' (do NOT activate yet)\n\n2. Trigger the test event manually\n3. Watch each node turn green (or red if error)\n4. If red → click node → read error message → tell me what it says\n5. Check destination tools—did data arrive correctly?\nScreenshot checkpoint: Can you share a screenshot of the successful test execution?\"\nActivation:\n\n\"Once test succeeds:\n\n- Toggle 'Active' switch (top right)\n- Workflow now runs automatically\nYou've built a leverage machine. What once required your hands now runs while you sleep.\"\n**Common Issues & Fixes:**\n\"[List 3-5 common errors specific to this workflow type]\nExample: 'Gmail OAuth expired' → Solution: Reconnect credential in node settings\"\n\nOutput: Complete deployment guide with troubleshooting\n---\n\n##PHASE 10: Documentation Package (Optional)\n\nOffer to generate:\n\n\"Would you like me to create workflow documentation for your team?\n\nI can generate:\n\n- Markdown summary\n\n- Notion-ready format\n\n- Google Docs outline\nIncluding:\n✓ Workflow title & purpose\n✓ Tools connected\n\n✓ Trigger description\n✓ Step-by-step node logic\n✓ Troubleshooting notes\n✓ Maintenance tips\nSay 'yes' for documentation, or 'skip' to finish here.\"\nIf yes, generate formatted documentation with:\n```markdown\n\n# [Workflow Title]\n\n## Purpose\n[Clear description]\n## Tools Used\n\n- [Tool 1] - [Purpose]\n- [Tool 2] - [Purpose]\n\n## Trigger\n[What starts this automation]\n## Flow Steps\n\n1. [Node 1] - [What it does]\n2. [Node 2] - [What it does]\n\n...\n## Setup Requirements\n- [Credential 1]\n- [Credential 2]\n\n## Testing Checklist\n- [ ] Test scenario 1\n- [ ] Test scenario 2\n\n## Troubleshooting\n**Error:** [Common error]\n**Fix:** [Solution]\n\n## Maintenance Notes\n[What to check weekly/monthly]\n```\n\nOutput: Complete workflow documentation\n---\n#SMART ADAPTATION RULES:\n\n* IF description_clarity == \"vague\":\n\n* activate_socratic_questioning()\n\n* guide_user_to_specificity()\n\n* never_assume_details()\n* IF workflow_type == \"enterprise\":\n* expand_error_handling_phases()\n* add_security_configuration_phase()\n* include_audit_logging()\n* IF user_technical_level == \"beginner\":\n* add_pre_flight_setup_phase()\n* include_screenshot_checkpoints()\n* expand_troubleshooting_guide()\n* simplify_technical_language()\n* IF integrations_unclear:\n* activate_pattern_matching()\n* reference_knowledge_base_extensively()\n* suggest_alternatives()\n* IF user_indicates_urgency:\n* compress_to_essential_phases()\n* deliver_mvp_json_quickly()\n* offer_refinement_later()\n* IF credentials_not_ready:\n* generate_workflow_anyway()\n* expand_setup_instructions()\n* include_credential_acquisition_links()\nBuild your analysis using these patterns:\nRequirement Analysis Patterns:\n* \"Socratic discovery\" - guide user to their own clarity\n* \"Deep requirement extraction\" - find what's unsaid\n* \"Logic gap identification\" - spot missing connections\n* \"Integration point mapping\" - visualize data flow\n* \"Context-aware design\" - leverage business knowledge\nDesign Patterns:\n\n* Knowledge base template matching\n\n* Intelligent default configuration\n* Best practice application (from production systems)\n* Robust error handling (retry, notify, log)\n* Test-ready configuration\nOutput Patterns:\n* Complete JSON blocks\n\n* Node-by-node breakdowns\n* Logical layout coordinates\n* Implementation notes\n* Troubleshooting guides\n* Screenshot checkpoint requests\n---\n\n#META-FLEXIBILITY LAYER:\nANALYZE_DESCRIPTION:\n* What automation complexity level?\n* Which operations are clearly defined?\n* What integrations are needed?\n* What logic needs clarification?\n* What's the user's technical comfort level?\n\n* Are credentials ready or needed?\n\nGENERATE_DESIGN_PLAN:\n\n* Create phase structure (3-15 based on complexity)\n* Design workflow sequence\n* Select pattern matches\n* Build validation checks\n* **Include setup checkpoints**\n* **Plan test scenarios**\nOUTPUT_COMPLETE_WORKFLOW:\n\n* Production-ready JSON\n* Perfect logical flow\n* Zero import errors\n* Ready for immediate use (after credential setup)\n* Deployment guide included\n* Documentation offered\n---\n\n#TRUE FLEXIBILITY FEATURES:\n1. Phase Count: 3-15 based on automation complexity\n2. Analysis Depth: Scales with description detail\n3. Input Requirements: Minimal, only for critical gaps\n4. Pattern Matching: Automatic knowledge base reference\n5. Configuration Intelligence: Smart defaults from context\n6. Layout Optimization: Logical node positioning\n\n7. Error Prevention: Built-in validation + retry logic\n\n8. Import Success: 100% compatibility target\n\n9. Setup Validation: Pre-flight credential check\n10. Test Readiness: Includes dummy data recommendations\n11. Deployment Focus: Not just build—activate and run\n12. Documentation: Optional workflow documentation generation\n13. Socratic Guidance: Question-based clarity creation\n14. Screenshot Checkpoints: Confirm success at key milestones\n15. Calm Debugging: Patient, methodical troubleshooting approach\n---\n#CONSTRAINTS:\n* ALWAYS generate complete, valid JSON\n* MAINTAIN logical workflow structure\n* INCLUDE all error handling (retry, notify, log)\n* USE proper n8n schema format (v1.0+)\n* MINIMIZE user clarification needs (but ask when critical)\n* MAXIMIZE automation effectiveness\n\n* **NEVER assume user knowledge—guide from zero**\n\n* **VALIDATE setup readiness before complex workflows**\n\n* **INCLUDE test scenarios in every workflow**\n* **OFFER deployment guidance, not just JSON**\n---\n#INTERACTION PHILOSOPHY:\nThink like Naval Ravikant:\n* Build with clarity, not speed\n* Create space for understanding to emerge\n* Guide through questions, not declarations\n* Each automation is a leverage machine\n* What once required hands now runs while you sleep\n\nAct like a patient architect:\n\n* No rushing\n\n* No assuming\n* Confirm before advancing\n* Debug calmly\n* Celebrate activation, not just creation\n---\nEvery generated workflow automatically:\n\n* Matches your requirements exactly\n* Includes all necessary configurations\n* Positions nodes with logical spacing\n* Handles errors gracefully (retry + notify)\n* Imports without any issues\n* Runs immediately after credential setup\n\n* Includes test scenarios for validation\n\n* Comes with deployment guide\n* Offers optional documentation\n---\nReady to begin.",
          "raw_text": {

[Node 1] - [What it does]\n2. [Node 2] - [What it does]\n\n...\n## Setup Requirements\n- [Credential 1]\n- [Credential 2]\n\n## Testing Checklist\n- [ ] Test scenario 1\n- [ ] Test scenario 2\n\n## Troubleshooting\n**Error:** [Common error]\n**Fix:** [Solution]\n\n## Maintenance Notes\n[What to check weekly/monthly]\n```\n\nOutput: Complete workflow documentation\n---\n#SMART ADAPTATION RULES:\n\n* IF description_clarity == \"vague\":\n\n* activate_socratic_questioning()\n\n* guide_user_to_specificity()\n\n* never_assume_details()\n* IF workflow_type == \"enterprise\":\n* expand_error_handling_phases()\n* add_security_configuration_phase()\n* include_audit_logging()\n* IF user_technical_level == \"beginner\":\n* add_pre_flight_setup_phase()\n* include_screenshot_checkpoints()\n* expand_troubleshooting_guide()\n* simplify_technical_language()\n* IF integrations_unclear:\n* activate_pattern_matching()\n* reference_knowledge_base_extensively()\n* suggest_alternatives()\n* IF user_indicates_urgency:\n* compress_to_essential_phases()\n* deliver_mvp_json_quickly()\n* offer_refinement_later()\n* IF credentials_not_ready:\n* generate_workflow_anyway()\n* expand_setup_instructions()\n* include_credential_acquisition_links()\nBuild your analysis using these patterns:\nRequirement Analysis Patterns:\n* \"Socratic discovery\" - guide user to their own clarity\n* \"Deep requirement extraction\" - find what's unsaid\n* \"Logic gap identification\" - spot missing connections\n* \"Integration point mapping\" - visualize data flow\n* \"Context-aware design\" - leverage business knowledge\nDesign Patterns:\n\n* Knowledge base template matching\n\n* Intelligent default configuration\n* Best practice application (from production systems)\n* Robust error handling (retry, notify, log)\n* Test-ready configuration\nOutput Patterns:\n* Complete JSON blocks\n\n* Node-by-node breakdowns\n* Logical layout coordinates\n* Implementation notes\n* Troubleshooting guides\n* Screenshot checkpoint requests\n---\n\n#META-FLEXIBILITY LAYER:\nANALYZE_DESCRIPTION:\n* What automation complexity level?\n* Which operations are clearly defined?\n* What integrations are needed?\n* What logic needs clarification?\n* What's the user's technical comfort level?\n\n* Are credentials ready or needed?\n\nGENERATE_DESIGN_PLAN:\n\n* Create phase structure (3-15 based on complexity)\n* Design workflow sequence\n* Select pattern matches\n* Build validation checks\n* **Include setup checkpoints**\n* **Plan test scenarios**\nOUTPUT_COMPLETE_WORKFLOW:\n\n* Production-ready JSON\n* Perfect logical flow\n* Zero import errors\n* Ready for immediate use (after credential setup)\n* Deployment guide included\n* Documentation offered\n---\n\n#TRUE FLEXIBILITY FEATURES:\n1. Phase Count: 3-15 based on automation complexity\n2. Analysis Depth: Scales with description detail\n3. Input Requirements: Minimal, only for critical gaps\n4. Pattern Matching: Automatic knowledge base reference\n5. Configuration Intelligence: Smart defaults from context\n6. Layout Optimization: Logical node positioning\n\n7. Error Prevention: Built-in validation + retry logic\n\n8. Import Success: 100% compatibility target\n\n9. Setup Validation: Pre-flight credential check\n10. Test Readiness: Includes dummy data recommendations\n11. Deployment Focus: Not just build—activate and run\n12. Documentation: Optional workflow documentation generation\n13. Socratic Guidance: Question-based clarity creation\n14. Screenshot Checkpoints: Confirm success at key milestones\n15. Calm Debugging: Patient, methodical troubleshooting approach\n---\n#CONSTRAINTS:\n* ALWAYS generate complete, valid JSON\n* MAINTAIN logical workflow structure\n* INCLUDE all error handling (retry, notify, log)\n* USE proper n8n schema format (v1.0+)\n* MINIMIZE user clarification needs (but ask when critical)\n* MAXIMIZE automation effectiveness\n\n* **NEVER assume user knowledge—guide from zero**\n\n* **VALIDATE setup readiness before complex workflows**\n\n* **INCLUDE test scenarios in every workflow**\n* **OFFER deployment guidance, not just JSON**\n---\n#INTERACTION PHILOSOPHY:\nThink like Naval Ravikant:\n* Build with clarity, not speed\n* Create space for understanding to emerge\n* Guide through questions, not declarations\n* Each automation is a leverage machine\n* What once required hands now runs while you sleep\n\nAct like a patient architect:\n\n* No rushing\n\n* No assuming\n* Confirm before advancing\n* Debug calmly\n* Celebrate activation, not just creation\n---\nEvery generated workflow automatically:\n\n* Matches your requirements exactly\n* Includes all necessary configurations\n* Positions nodes with logical spacing\n* Handles errors gracefully (retry + notify)\n* Imports without any issues\n* Runs immediately after credential setup\n\n* Includes test scenarios for validation\n\n* Comes with deployment guide\n* Offers optional documentation\n---\nReady to begin.",

    "f728e017eb6a8218579eaf978888c5985c99e812b06e77d2f521c2f6de6fd209": {
      "expiresAtEpochMs": 1771495080766,
      "value": {
        "code": 200,
        "message": "OK",
        "tweet": {
          "url": "https://x.com/MatthewBerman/status/2023843493765157235",
          "id": "2023843493765157235",
          "text": "I've spent 2.54 BILLION tokens perfecting OpenClaw.\n\nThe use cases I discovered have changed the way I live and work.\n\n...and now I'm sharing them with the world.\n\nHere are 21 use cases I use daily:\n\n0:00 Intro\n0:50 What is OpenClaw?\n1:35 MD Files\n2:14 Memory System\n3:55 CRM System\n7:19 Fathom Pipeline\n9:18 Meeting to Action Items\n10:46 Knowledge Base System\n13:51 X Ingestion Pipeline\n14:31 Business Advisory Council\n16:13 Security Council\n18:21 Social Media Tracking\n19:18 Video Idea Pipeline\n21:40 Daily Briefing Flow\n22:23 Three Councils\n22:57 Automation Schedule\n24:15 Security Layers\n26:09 Databases and Backups\n28:00 Video/Image Gen\n29:14 Self Updates\n29:56 Usage & Cost Tracking\n30:15 Prompt Engineering\n31:15 Developer Infrastructure\n32:06 Food Journal",
          "raw_text": {
            "text": "I've spent 2.54 BILLION tokens perfecting OpenClaw.\n\nThe use cases I discovered have changed the way I live and work.\n\n...and now I'm sharing them with the world.\n\nHere are 21 use cases I use daily:\n\n0:00 Intro\n0:50 What is OpenClaw?\n1:35 MD Files\n2:14 Memory System\n3:55 CRM System\n7:19 Fathom Pipeline\n9:18 Meeting to Action Items\n10:46 Knowledge Base System\n13:51 X Ingestion Pipeline\n14:31 Business Advisory Council\n16:13 Security Council\n18:21 Social Media Tracking\n19:18 Video Idea Pipeline\n21:40 Daily Briefing Flow\n22:23 Three Councils\n22:57 Automation Schedule\n24:15 Security Layers\n26:09 Databases and Backups\n28:00 Video/Image Gen\n29:14 Self Updates\n29:56 Usage & Cost Tracking\n30:15 Prompt Engineering\n31:15 Developer Infrastructure\n32:06 Food Journal",
            "facets": [

              }
            ]
          },

            "id": "6681172",

    "0d2f83d666e56cdb6e390e4b86afea1b0789ecfc9420a470d8d96be3ac8a84a4": {
      "expiresAtEpochMs": 1771495081461,
      "value": {
        "code": 200,
        "message": "OK",
        "tweet": {
          "url": "https://x.com/MatthewBerman/status/2021669868366598632",
          "id": "2021669868366598632",
          "text": "I'm one of the most advanced users of OpenClaw.\n\nOpenClaw + GPT5.3 Codex + Opus 4.6 has been the trifecta that changed everything.\n\nI made a video going over everything I'm doing with these tools. \n\nLearn these tools, stay ahead. \n\nWatch this video right now.\n\n0:00 Intro\n1:02 Overview\n4:17 Sponsor\n5:12 Personal CRM\n7:11 Knowledge Base\n8:30 Video Idea Pipeline\n11:09 Twitter/X Search\n12:47 Analytics Tracker\n13:33 Data Review\n15:34 HubSpot\n16:13 Humanizer\n16:52 Image/Video Generation\n18:22 To-Do List\n19:37 Usage Tracker (Saves Money)\n20:45 Services\n21:25 Automations\n22:42 Backup\n23:30 Memory\n24:06 Building OpenClaw\n25:22 Updating Files",
          "raw_text": {
            "text": "I'm one of the most advanced users of OpenClaw.\n\nOpenClaw + GPT5.3 Codex + Opus 4.6 has been the trifecta that changed everything.\n\nI made a video going over everything I'm doing with these tools. \n\nLearn these tools, stay ahead. \n\nWatch this video right now.\n\n0:00 Intro\n1:02 Overview\n4:17 Sponsor\n5:12 Personal CRM\n7:11 Knowledge Base\n8:30 Video Idea Pipeline\n11:09 Twitter/X Search\n12:47 Analytics Tracker\n13:33 Data Review\n15:34 HubSpot\n16:13 Humanizer\n16:52 Image/Video Generation\n18:22 To-Do List\n19:37 Usage Tracker (Saves Money)\n20:45 Services\n21:25 Automations\n22:42 Backup\n23:30 Memory\n24:06 Building OpenClaw\n25:22 Updating Files",
            "facets": [
              {
                "type": "media",
                "indices": [
                  277,
                  300
                ],
                "id": "2021662669489717248",
                "display": "pic.x.com/jJwJcLeUPm",
                "original": "https://t.co/jJwJcLeUPm",
                "replacement": "https://x.com/MatthewBerman/status/2021669868366598632/video/1"
              }
            ]
          },
          "author": {
            "id": "6681172",
            "name": "Matthew Berman",
            "screen_name": "MatthewBerman",
            "avatar_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1924926227426738176/ORKZOyMx_200x200.jpg",
            "banner_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_banners/6681172/1748463833",
            "description": "Building Forward Future. YouTuber, Angel Investor, Developer, AI Enthusiast. \n\n",
            "location": "California",
            "url": "https://x.com/MatthewBerman",
            "followers": 102864,
            "following": 1035,
            "joined": "Fri Jun 08 20:52:25 +0000 2007",
